本作为纪念《罗德斯岛战记》系列诞生30周年而制作的纪念作品。游戏剧情描写了女主角蒂德莉特在小说《誓约之宝冠》故事发生前的一段“空白时期”的故事，并在原作小说作者水野良的监督下制作。

12時間でクリア。 濃密なディードリット版、メトロイドヴァニア。  ネタゲーではなく完成度よく好印象で面白かったです。 丁寧な説明と徐々に強くなっていくゲーム性、見たことのあるボス達、 遊びがいがあって先が気になる作りがとてもいい。 ボリュームもそこそこなのでダレる事なく遊びやすい。 セーブポイントを小まめにしないとやられた場合、戻されるのでそこは注意。 あと、画面中央上にある左右のバーに慣れる事がこのゲームの鍵。 感覚的には20時間ぐらい遊んでいたような充実感があって人にもおすすめしたい作品。  自分は久々にOVA版のアニメを見返したりして気分を上げたりしていました。

I fondly remember watching the Lodoss War anime as a kid and was very excited to see this game pop up in my recommendations. It's an after-the-ending sort of story, rather like Frieren: Beyond Journey's End, and is technically full of spoilers for Parn's own adventure to become the Knight of Lodoss, but anybody familiar with fantasy tropes won't be shocked and this game can still be enjoyed by anybody.  The Metroidvania design is drawn directly from Castlevania: Symphony of the Night without really any of the hardcore combat mechanics which have migrated into this genre over recent years, so in that sense Wonder Labyrinth is a throwback to simpler times. Never fear, though, there is a steady flow of new weapons and magic spells keeping the moment-to-moment fresh, and Deedlit learns enough traversal skills to make her feel satisfyingly nimble. Character sprites are big and beautiful, with some new enemy type around every corner, and the variety of boss encounters are great too. For only 12 hours, I enjoyed every moment of scouring the map for secrets, and it helps that the soundtrack is equally delightful.  As an aside, I've seen some people have trouble finding the unlockable Boss Rush mode. Rather than being added to the main menu, it appears as a new option when you load your save, because Deedlit's leveling carries over between the two modes. If it feels too hard at first, do some exploring and grinding in the story mode, buy some items if needed, then try again. And pay attention to the elemental affinity system!

I'm more of a sci-fi person, so it was a surprise that Record in Lodoss War is one of the very few fantasy worlds that hooked me. Thanks to that though, this also now ended up being one of my favorite metroidvanias.   It doesn't really do anything spectacular, it just excels in its polish. I ran into nary a single bug, never felt like I died in a fight that was out of my control or didn't feel like my fault, and every mechanic throughout was easy to grasp and felt useful to engage with. My only critique would probably be the one a lot of genre veterans have - it's rather easy. Even as someone who's not played a ton of these types of games, it was pretty easy to clear most bosses, with the final one being a pushover compared to others.  Despite that, it is 1000% worth the playthrough. I highly [i]highly[/i] recommend at least watching the first Lodoss War series before playing this to really get satisfaction from the references and nods, but even without the context, Team Ladybug nailed this - the game is a delight.
Without knowing Record of Lodoss War you can enjoy it anyway, really fun metroidvania, the equipment its really funny to get and the exploration gives a nice "lost" experience

《蒂德莉特的奇境冒险》是一场玩家在像素迷宫中对经典动漫的温柔回望。  《蒂德莉特的奇境冒险》用类恶魔城的躯壳，承载了精灵们属性切换的灵魂，每一次风火精灵之力的转换，不仅是破解关卡的钥匙，更是对原著中“风与炎之部族”寓言的交互式吟唱。  《蒂德莉特的奇境冒险》以精炼的流程，将蒂德莉特那份“永恒的少女”的孤独与追寻，化为一场面向内心的短途朝圣，同时也是对传统剑与魔法世界观中“寿命论”题材的一种表达。  《蒂德莉特的奇境冒险》虽然总体量偏向轻量，属于原著粉丝向作品，但依旧能让老玩家在熟悉的旋律中回忆，同时也让新玩家能瞥见那个剑与魔法时代的辽阔余晖。
继承了ladybug优秀的像素美术，游戏流程偏短质量对于粉丝向作品来说只能算及格，对我来说更多是情怀加成，小时候在影像店就被蒂朵跟op给硬控，童年女神这一块

Record of Lodoss War-Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth is a really fun metroidvania game. What makes it unique is that you have two different elements you can switch between at any point. Some enemies are weak to one of them, so you are constantly switching between them.   For combat, you have melee, ranged with a bow, and ranged with magic. The pixel art in this game is very fun and the metroidvania upgrades you get along the way are all fun to use. If you like metroidvania games, this is a must play. Also the music has some pretty good bass riffs.

[h1] A visually appealing metroidvania [/h1]  I wasn't familiar with the Record of Lodoss War before I played the game. I presumed the game to be a standalone title. Apparently the world and story deepens an already existing universe that has been around for quite a while. I'm not knowledgeable on the series and will not take a stand on how this compares to other titles or the overarching story. Instead I will give my two cents from my simple perspective of liking games of the genre.  The immediate aspect I laid my eyes upon was the visual polish in pixel art. Color schemes and palettes between the characters and surroundings are composed and soothing. There are very little overly saturated or bright colors and tones. Everything flows and blends naturally with one another. I have two different ideas or explanations of my own for why this is. I have to remind right now that I have no knowledge whatsoever of this universe and how it works. If I did, maybe my interpretation would change. As far as I know our protagonist Deedlit is an elf. Typically elves live in harmony with nature and the chosen palette reflects in my opinion just that, Deedlit's connection to nature and their place in it.  Yet the harmony is at an imbalance...  Deedlit pursues their lover Parn in a labyrinth and in this combat filled venture is interrupted a multitude of times with dialog that question the surrounding reality. Visions of the past or of a different timeline invades Deedlit's mind and the curtain starts to tear with every new encounter. Characters that once were hostile start to guide our protagonist in the slowly rising insight of what everything really stands for. In the end Deedlit comes to understand that they were in denial of the loss of Parn. In grief Deedlit fell in past memories and lived them over and over in hopes to be once again united with their one. And this dreamlike made up reality gives forth my second explanation for the colors. The haziness throughout the environments and their colors, makes connections to the dreamworld and to whom it belongs, that has entrapped the mourning Deedlit.  I lied. There is a third reason. It looks beautiful! [hr][/hr]  Now for something completely different and more straightforward. You have two different stances or spirits (Sylph/wind and Salamander/fire) that you can switch freely in any given instant to alter your attacks and movement. Some enemies are immune to the other and some areas require you to frequently swap between the spirits in rhythm. Both of them come in beautiful visual effects in movement and attacking. Maybe a minor point for some, but this was one of the first notes I took while playing. The tracers left behind when sprinting or the ground taking impact from the breeze left behind from levitating are both great elements that elevate the feeling of interacting with the world. Visual effects in general seem to have attention put into them. Reality bends have been done well to feel like a pulling motion on the screen while still staying mostly in two dimensions. There are a few 3D animated objects sprinkled every now and then that still retain the 2D feel which is cool. Hats off to visual direction in every way possible!  And last, a note for usability and accessibility! I don't recommend playing with keyboard and mouse, as I wouldn't with any other metroidvania as the intuitive movement is often lost if not played with controller. However, getting the game to recognize your controller can be a pain in the ass. Seems that the game registers only Xbox 360 controllers, and I had to find an way around this as I play with PS5 controller. I masked my controller as an different one with DS4/5windows software. It's not hard to manage, but takes away from the joy of just wanting to begin with a new game. Take this into consideration before you begin, if you are going to play with controller! [hr][/hr]  To put it short. I liked it. Could've been a bit more challenging and longer, but in any case worth to play. I had fun! Soundtrack was also on par with metroidvanias. Didn't reach the levels of the greats, but still.  Thanks for taking the time, means much <3

Le jeu ne réinvente rien, mais c'est pas ce qu'on attendait d'une licence aussi vieille qui, dans les années 90, était un anime et un manga assez important -- Donc ça fait très plaisir de le voir en jeu sur Steam !  Ici, on incarne Deedlit, l'elfe d'un groupe d'aventurier qui combat le mal et les démons. Le récit prend place bien après leurs aventures, bien après la mort du héros -- et pourtant celui-ci est encore en vie, ainsi que tous les démons vaincu par le groupe. Tous sont piégés dans le Labyrinthe. L'objectif est donc double : vaincre les forces maléfiques à nouveau et comprendre les mystères de ce lieux.  Cela dit ce n'est pas un anime/manga que j'ai connu à l'époque, je suis juste parti lire quelques fiches pour avoir les bases du scénario. J'ai compris le scénario du jeu mais il ne m'a pas marqué.  Niveau gameplay en revanche on s'amuse puisqu'il reprend clairement Symphony of the Night ; des murs cachés, une centaine d'arme à obtenir chez les marchands ou en tuant des monstres, des objets, des sorts de magie, des boss, et un système de pouvoir sympa. Vous maitrisez le feu et le vent ; l'un est fort contre l'autre, et utiliser du feu contre du feu (ou vent contre vent) vous en immunise. Tous les monstres du jeu on un pouvoir également qu'il faudra contrer. Pareil pour les sorts, ils vous confèrent différentes aptitudes utiles en combat.  Bien sûr graphiquement ça claque du poney, c'est super beau, mais ça vous pouvez déjà le voir dans les images plus haut. Les animations son nickelles. La musique années 90 et les effets sonores rétro c'est la cerise sur le gateau. Et en plus on voit les dégâts infligés donc moi j'ai qu'une envie : faire plus de dégâts !  C'est un super bon jeu avec un super bel enrobage. En fait, si vous chercher un Metroidvania avec une vibe rétro, il est juste parfait.

Como fan de la franquicia Lodoss, y fan de Metroidvanias, debo decir que esta ha sido una experiencia fantástica. Visualmente es una preciosidad, con un pixel art muy buen hecho y detallado, especialmente los de los jefes. el combate es bastante disfrutable, siendo muy rememorativo a Simphony of the Night, aunque con una mecánica muy única como la de Sylph y Salamander, que añaden un toque especial al combate y exploración, junto con el muy bueno uso de los puzzle con el arco. la historia, pese a que no es la gran maravilla, cumple bastante bien en justificar toda la odisea, y resulta mas especial si ya haz visto el anime o algún otro medio de la franquicia. si tuviese que decir aspectos negativos, hablaría de la música, que no es mala, e incluso tiene temas que me gustan, pero no es del todo memorable, y mucho menos comparable a juegos como los Castlevania, y sobre todo, su duración, que haciendo las cosas bien, puedes completar todo el juego al 100% en mas o menos 5 o 6 horas. Pese a esas contras, es un juego excelente, 100% recomendable si te gustan los Metroidvainas o si te gusto el anime.

A really fun and quick metroidvania taking heavy inspiration from Symphony of the Night...    The main gimmick, outside of traditional explore -> new power -> boss fight loop, is the ability to switch elements between wind and fire at any time; being of one element makes you immune to those attacks, and also charges the opposite element; being fully charged in a single element allows you to heal over time. This makes combat a fast-paced kind of dance, where you quickly switch back and forth between elements to dodge, heal, deal effective damage, etc., and you can really feel how fast and agile Deedlit is as a character.  The game doesn't wear out its welcome, I completed it 100% in about 7-8 hours, and it was a top tier indie experience. Definitely give it a shot!

The game feels great. Moving around and jumping is just right. There are a lot of different weapons to find and use. The game is based on an anime from 1990 called Record of Lodoss War. I haven’t seen it so the story didn’t make sense to me.   The main thing of this game is the two magic system, wind and fire. You can switch between wind and fire. Some enemies can’t be hit by one. You don’t take damage by the same element so when bosses use both you switch to the right one to avoid taking a lot of damage. Getting hit the same element absorbs it into your magic meter to refill it a bit. There are 8 or so spells and I only found the first one useful and used it the whole game. The elements can be level 1, 2, or 3. The higher it is the more damage the main weapon does with that element. While the active one is 3 you slowly heal. It goes down 1 when you take damage. It goes up a bit when you beat an enemy with the other element active. It makes you want to switch often to keep both elements high for damage and healing.  --------The rest is what I don’t like-------- Full reset to last save on game over. I beat a boss and died right after so I had to do the boss again. I don’t like when games do this.  You are stunned for so stupid long. It gets extra annoying in bosses where the damage is high and you can’t move for a while.  Trying to hit the gear with the bow to make the platform that you are on move wouldn’t be that bad if it was one way. You can make the gear go backwards and there’s never a single time you want to do that. It’s hard to hit and annoying to hit the right side of the gear while on the moving platform.   The last area was just filled with only high level enemies that took so many hits and did so much damage. A big jump from the area before. At least the last area is pretty short but it was still pretty frustrating to get through.   Usually boss rushes are bonus content (and there is in this game) but there is also a required boss rush at the end of the game. It’s so long and so many times I failed at the last few bosses. You get a full heal each boss but the later bosses are bit hard and takes 5 minutes or more to get back to them only to slip up a bit and have to start over.

依旧优秀的像素美术、新颖的能力设计和勾石的地图设计。  整体难度较低，一周目流程十小时不到，手感中规中矩，算是这个公司制作的类银里手感相对较好的一部了，双属性切换的设计挺有意思的，在地图设计和怪物设计上也有对应双属性切换的机制，整体体验还不错。  地图设计依旧拉胯，区域与区域之间连通性很差，开了捷径但因为传送点位置的原因使用率缺很低，属于无效捷径。前几章地图至少还有设计性，有几个弓箭解谜玩起来挺有意思，但到后期地图基本变成《东方夜神月》那样的塞怪摆烂式地图，最终Boss前还搞个BossRush也真是没活了。地图“锁”的设计过度依赖颜色钥匙门，对滑铲的应用甚至只有两处，对类银能力锁的应用浅尝辄止。  人物性能一般，转向操作比较僵硬，受击以后会有个很大的击退，受击过多或者被特殊属性攻击打中还会出现眩晕机制，比如最终Boss一阶段僧侣形态的三连电击，只要挨到一下就会被眩晕吃下后面的几下，可能会直接暴毙。  UI设计比较简陋，地图没法放大缩小也没法做标记，对探索不是很友好。角色道具算在技能里我觉得是个很抽象的设计，每次技能盘转半天才能摸到自己想用的技能或者道具。这个UI也是被套用了在这个制作组好几个作品了，说实话字体设计看的真的很累我不是很喜欢。  Boss设计里非人形Boss设计的都还不错，但人形Boss基本都很烂，通过薄纱主角的机动性把主角按在地上打，依靠主角羸弱的机动性真的很难躲，基本只能靠无脑法术灌伤的拒绝交互式打法才能打过，我个人觉得这种战斗模式有点畸形（包括这公司其他作品也是这种玩法）。  整体探索体验还行，算是个中规中矩的作品，如果对原作剧情有了解体验会更好一些。     ps1. 有个地方的解谜要扫二维码，初见的时候还挺有意思的。 ps2. 地牢里那些爬墙上的大蜈蚣大蜘蛛真是给我看的汗流浃背了，害怕节肢动物的真看不得这个……

[h1]总体相当一般。[/h1]  优点： 整体制作看着还比较精良，美术风格讨喜。 角色元素设定机制新颖，体验不错 难度中等(不算难)，拿到隐身技能之后可以在boss战中逃课，降低难度  缺点：地图设计不太行，跑路非常费劲，传送点的位置怪异的很，个别区域没内容但是堆了十几个怪，强行降低跑图 战斗手感欠缺优化，容易在战斗时不能及时转身导致操作变形 被一些怪打到会出现非常离谱的击退效果，直接飞出地图导致区域重刷 眩晕机制感觉很蠢，除了增加boss把你一套带走的概率没卵用，对游戏没任何正面提升
打分：8.5/10  非常意外的一部作品。  感觉Team Ladybug在类银河战士恶魔城游戏的设计上具有十足的巧思与独到的心得，可以和Kyle Thompson坐一桌。  PS： （1）打完boss一定要记得回头存档。  （2）打完boss比萝蒂丝之后陷入回忆之中，放四个不死之王只能躲没有任何反制手段的一本道是整个游戏最傻逼的设计之一，删掉这个情节游戏就会完美很多。

Record of Lodoss War: Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth - Playtime 4.7 hours  This is a neat little game I guess.  It's a half decent metroidvania game and it has a couple of nice ideas.  The Ikaruga mechanic of switching your elements to avoid things and having each element be better at certain things is nice.  The fact that they both increase in power based on combat with the other element helps with making you switch between them more, and by the end of the game you get really used to switching on the fly to get the most of what you need.  It felt nice to switch from fire in wind just in time to use the wind element's float ability to avoid an attack, but by the time I started doing that sort of tech it felt like the game was basically over.    The game is pretty short, and feels extremely streamlined compared to other metroidvanias.  It's basically to the point where it's almost just normal levels, there's very little to side track you or make you back track.  There's a number of secrets, but they don't feel too nice to get, as half the time it's just some equipment that you out scale really quickly.  On one hand, I kinda prefer levels, so i don't mid a streamlined game, but there were a lot of times where it really felt like I was just put in a hallway to fight a bunch of enemies that weren't that engaging.  The story is pretty bad.  It's about as bare bones as it gets, essentially doing the bare minimum possible to get fans of the series to point at the screen and go "OOOOH OOOOH REFERENCE YEAH!"  I never watched the series, but I still get the feeling that if I had I would feel kinda empty. It's just fighting a bunch of stuff I assume was in the series for the bare minimum reason, it sorta takes the wind out of your sails.  This game made me wonder if the developer's previous game Touhou Luna Nights was as good as I remembered it, or if I just wasn't noticing it when I played that game, which is never a place you want to be.  I think Luna Nights was decently better, but thinking about it it definitely had the same story and streamlining problems now that I think about it (though i don't particularly mind the stream lining in all honesty).  I think the game was a bit harder than this game is, which did alot for it being better.  Overall it's fun enough, but I'd definitely wait for a decent sale if you really wanted to try it, it's simply not enough for anything more that $10 imo.  I'll probably still check out their other new games eventually, as I think they've still got it, just not really in this game.

Cara, é uma pena eu dar essa análise negativa pra este jogo que eu amei até uns 80% dele. A gameplay, estrutura do jogo, design de armaduras e armas, direção de arte são muito bons.  A história eu achei uma bela de uma MERDA até onde eu joguei. Completamente tosco, o jogo não te da nenhuma introdução de nada. Só fica fazendo uns flashbacks bem ruins pra tentar explicar algo que não tem sentido algum. Parece que era pra eu saber alguma coisa sobre o jogo antes de jogar pra fazer sentido... sei lá.. Mas esse problema da história nem foi o pior, pra mim o jeito que o jogo escala a dificuldade de forma artificial a partir do penúltimo capítulo que foi a gota d'água. Eu jogo para me divertir, e este jogo consegue te tirar do sério com alguns desafios insuportáveis e mal feitos.  ATENÇÃO SPOILERS ABAIXO:    1. Num determinado momento que você precisa mover umas plataformas atirando com o arco nas engrenagens. Que coisa ridícula cara... muito mal feito pois a mecânica do jogo é tão ruim que deixa difícil sem a menor necessidade.  2. Aquela parte que a personagem morre e fica num mundo dos sonhos onde você não tem armas e tem que ficar esquivando evitando que os fantasmas te ataquem. É completamente aleatório, não tem uma forma de passar daquilo sem ser na sorte.  3. Na última fase simplesmente começam a colocar um monte de inimigos gigantes juntos em espaços confinados e a dificuldade escala de uma forma que não faz o menor sentido. É realmente simplesmente aumentar a vida, hitbox, ataque e velocidade dos inimigos.   Se você não se importa em se frustrar em alguns momentos e morrer bastante repetidamente (nem adianta vir com o argumento do Dark Souls pois eu platinei o 1 e o 3 (esse no ps5)) talvez você goste, mas eu não posso recomendar algo que não me divertiu.

純粋にアクションゲームとしての感想として書くと、アクション仕様が未熟ゆえに 思った通りのアクション操作ができないのが残念。 特に挙げられるのが下記二点。 ・攻撃モーション中にスライディングの選考入力すると何故かジャンプが発動する 　（スライディングモーション中は無敵判定なので回避として使えるが、誤爆でジャンプし被弾する） ・攻撃モーションが終わってから数コンマ待たないと方向転換されない 　（敵に囲まれた際に背面の敵に攻撃をしようとしても、少し後ろに下がり前方に攻撃する） そのためモーションが完全終了してからでないと次の狙った操作ができないため 誤爆のオンパレードとなる。 特にやりこみ要素の「一発即死モード」はこの仕様のせいでやれる気がしない。  あと弓パズルギミックなんで入れたの？。面白くないし意味ないしイライラするだけだよ。 （特にエレベーター）  もう少しアクションゲーム作成に慣れてるメーカーへ作成依頼したほうが良いと思う。

Análise depois de zerar o jogo, pode conter [b] spoilers. [/b]  [b] História e Narrativa [/b] Como a maioria que acredito que se interessou pelo jogo, eu tinha assistido o anime original a qual amo desde quando conheci na infância em um VHS! A história com certeza vai ser bem confusa para quem não conhece o universo e personagens da obra original, talvez faltando um cuidado para deixar ela apresentavel legal para novos conhecedores.  [b] Gameplay [/b] O bom e velho Castlevania, mas com mecânicas divertidas de intercalar ataques de arma corpo a corpo e arco a distância. Nada muito dificil, e com um ou outro boss que se precisa pegar um pouco do padrão e saber como spamar ataque.  [b] Desafio e Progressão [/b] Com um mapa que te faz ir e voltar pelas mesmas áreas várias vezes, e inimigos irritantes em areas de buracos ou apertadas. Alguns personagens aliados fornecem opções extras de ganhar dinheiro e itens, mas são bem opcionais para progressão. O jogo não é de nem longe dificil, e pode ser zerado até mesmo por quem não é acostumado com o estilo acredito eu.   [b] Gráficos e Direção de Arte [/b] Pixel art linda e bem detalhada, os inimigos são bem variados e dificilmente são só recolors. Soube recriar os personagens clássicos de forma muito fiel, mesmo com o minimalismo do pixel art. Cenários, skills, boss, todos impecaveis, se mexer estraga.  [b] Trilha Sonora e Áudio [/b] Um ponto que me surpreendeu muito, as músicas não tem inspiração no anime, mas são ótimas, com o uso de diversos instrumentos e que fazem balançar seu corpo em algumas fases.  [b] Bugs [/b] Nenhum, bom, as vezes eu achava meio sem sentido a colisão de alguns monstros com a personagem, em uma distância que na minha tela estava distante..., mas pode ser skill issue só.  [b] Conclusão [/b] Jogo extremamente curto, da pra finalizar em uma tarde. Divertido em tudo que um metroidvania deve ser, e muito nostalgico para os fãs da obra original. Vale a pena em uma promoção boa de valor. Uma nota final de 8/10.

Very enjoyable game, although I don't know if I'd say it has enough content to be worth $20. It has some lovely aesthetics and Deedlit is very fun to control, especially at the end. The game has an element system, where enemies are strong or weak to some, with Fire and Wind applying to Deedlit directly and the others mostly exclusive to certain spells. As you might expect, Fire makes Deedlit more powerful (in particular giving her a more mobile slide that can go through enemies), and Wind increasing jump height and allowing for hovering. Deedlit is immune to attacks of whichever element is currently equipped. Most bosses attack with mixes of the two, and they were mostly designed well enough to make me really consider what element I'm in. That said the game was generally very easy and with how quickly you reach one boss from the next. I would've liked it if they required more deliberation. I didn't need potions until some of the very last fights, and for the most part bosses don't need much strategy or pattern recognition to get through.

[h2][b]Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth: Uma Carta de Amor a "Record of Lodoss War"[/b][/h2]   [b]Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth[/b]- É um jogo que chega com uma mistura inusitada e com a missão dupla de agradar aos fãs da clássica série de light novels/anime [b]Record of Lodoss War[/b] e aos amantes do gênero [b]Metroidvania[/b].  [h3][b][u]Pontos Altos[/u][/b][/h3]  [i]Jogabilidade Fluida e Precisa:[/i]  Achei o combate um dos pontos altos do jogo. A movimentação de [b]Deedlit[/b] é extremamente ágil, e o sistema de armas (espadas, arcos e lanças, etc) é variado e responsivo. A mecânica principal envolve alternar entre os elementos de fogo e vento para causar dano eficiente em inimigos (que possuem fraquezas elementares) e, mais importante, para absorver ataques inimigos do elemento oposto, recuperando vida. Isso cria um fluxo de combate dinâmico e tático que para mim não ficou enjoativo.  [i]Arte e Trilha Sonora Encantadoras:[/i]  Visualmente, o jogo excelente. O estilo pixel art da equipe é primoroso, com cenários detalhados, animações de personagens muito fluidas e efeitos de partículas bonitos. A direção de arte captura perfeitamente a essência fantástica do Anime.   A trilha sonora, repleta de arranjos nostálgicos da obra original, é um presente para os fãs e cria uma atmosfera imersiva mesmo para quem não conhece a franquia.  [i]Level Design Inteligente:[/i]  Os cenários são bem construídos, com salas interconectadas de forma lógica. A exploração é recompensadora, e as habilidades elementares (fogo e vento) funcionam como as clássicas "habilidades de progressão" do gênero, abrindo novos caminhos de forma satisfatória à medida que você avança. O jogo também oferece um bom número de segredos e itens colecionáveis.  [i] Uma bela Homenagem aos Fãs: [/i]  Para quem cresceu assistindo ao [b]Anime[/b] ou lendo as [b]Novels[/b], o jogo é um prato cheio. A presença dos personagens icônicos e a exploração de elementos da rica mitologia de [b]Lodoss[/b] são inseridas de forma orgânica na narrativa do labirinto, criando uma ponte interessante com o material original.  [h3] [b] [u] Pontos Baixos [/u][/b][/h3]  [i]Duração e Curva de Dificuldade: [/i]  Eu só vou salientar esse ponto e que pode ser para alguns... para mim nao foi taaaanto assim. O principal ponto negativo pode ser é a [B]DURAÇÃO[/b]. A campanha principal pode ser concluída em cerca de 5 a 7 horas, o que pode parecer curto, especialmente para o [b]preço cheio[/b].   Além disso, a dificuldade é um tanto [b]Irregular[/b]: o jogo começou desafiador, mas conforme fui adquirindo e passei a entender o sistema [b]Elemental[/b], a segunda metade se torna consideravelmente mais fácil, diminuindo a tensão.  E por fim... [b]Vale a Pena?[/b]  👉 [spoiler][b]Sim[/b], definitivamente vale a pena.[/spoiler] 👈  Se você é fã de [b]Record of Lodoss War[/b], esta é uma experiência obrigatória e um tributo feito com muito carinho.   Se você é um entusiasta de [b]Metroidvanias[/b] que busca um jogo com combate refinado, visual deslumbrante e progressão sólida, também encontrará muito o que aproveitar.  Jogadores que buscam uma experiência longa e épica, com uma narrativa profunda, podem se sentir insatisfeitos com a duração e a simplicidade da história.  [b]Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth[/b] é como uma refeição deliciosa, mas de tamanho reduzido: a qualidade dos ingredientes é altíssima, o sabor é memorável, mas você termina desejando um pouco mais. É uma excelente aventura que acerta em cheio no combate e na arte.  Para mim, foi uma grata surpresa além de bem satisfatório... vale a pena conferir. =)

Nice fantasy Metroidvania platformer obviously inspired by the classic Castlevania: Symphony of the Night, but not as good or rich in content. Then again, it might be nostalgia misleading me. Not familiar at all with the Record of Lodoss War fiction, but I was intrigued by the story anyway. I'd argue I liked it more because I did not know what had happened before. A warning not to play this if you ever intend to read the novels or watch the anime. Myself, it made me want to watch it afterwards. I liked how the heroine moved as she felt responsive. The mechanic of switching between wind and fire affinity was well exploited and enjoyable. I was glad it wasn't between more than two, but you do eventually get more spells using other elements.  The soundtrack was good and mostly appropriate but nowhere near as great or memorable as Symphony. Very good animation for the heroine, less noticeable for the enemies. Beautiful pixel art. I especially liked the emphasis on female classical statues. There was a recurring bug where my controller kept vibrating until I quit the game. A minor problem was not able to look up and down (like Hollow Knight) so I could know what was coming. Changing powers and items became clumsy as the inventory became fuller. I was surprised there was no difficulty levels, as the only one veered to the easy side except for the later chapters and bosses. I didn't mind and enjoyed the progression and exploration.

Сложно судить. Прошёл, но в итоге не насладился. Скорее минус, чем плюс.  Играбельно, прошёл, но есть бесячие минусы: - в игре 6 актов, первые 5 ты словно в Hello Kitty играешь, включая боссов, но в 6 акте резко задирают сложность воткнув толпы толстых и тяжких для убийства мобов, с самонаводящимися выстрелами, летающими над огромными пропастями, улетающими в текстуры стен и т.п. - нет "рывка" - есть отскок, но он бесполезен чуть менее чем полностью - сзади либо конец платформы, либо шипы, либо и то и то. Многие боссы и мобы бьют дальше отскока - ожидание конца анимации перед следующим действием. Например, нельзя сделать "подкат" вперёд в бою, надо перестать бить, подождать, затем присесть, подождать и только затем катнуться вперёд. очень долго, не для боя, только для нескольких мест прохода - этот подкат как заявлено должен играть роль "рывка" с неуязвимостью, но помимо сложности его применения в бою, многие противники либо бьют далеко, либо слишком большие, чтобы полностью их проскочить - ОГЛУШЕНИЕ, максимально дебильная вещь. у ГГ много ХП и урон в большинстве случаев держит хорошо, но в определённый момент, причём я не понял что к этому приводит, но вам могут нанести оглушение, ГГ стоит как дура с летающими звёздочками в ступоре, а враг продолжает по вам наваливать, снова и снова, вы снова улетаете в оглушение и так повторяется, несколько раз чуть ли не с фулХП из-за оглушения в итоге улетал на респ - медленное всё - героиня, стрельба с лука, диалоги, кат-сцены, переключение фаз боссов у вас просто отбирают управление и надо ждать, пропустить и ускорить нельзя, диалоги можно проматывать но по одному "баблу" (возможно целиком, но тогда сюжет мимо вовсе) - на фул ачивки надо задрачивать, не скил, а имено гринд - тонны мобов на золото, тонны мобов на выбить оружие с микрошансом. Камон, я за всю игру только 2 раза выбил оружие с монстров, и то второй раз на платформе куда не мог тогда попасть без двойного прыжка, так что и не подобрал в итоге. А надо выбить вроде целую кучу с разных мобов, шанс там видать совсем никчёмный - НЕПРОПУСКАЕМЫЕ, мать их, ТИТРЫ в конце, за такое надо бить, пропустить нельзя, ускорения при удержании кнопок тоже не заметил. Минут 5 после финала текст крутится и ещё "сцена после титров", прежде чем можно выйти как положено, а не прибив процесс игры.

Avaliação: Muito Bom. Apesar do nome gigantesco, a duração é curtinha. Mas não se engane, pois o jogo é muito divertido!   A protagonista domina 2 elementos: Fogo e Vento. Eles são cruciais tanto para avançar nos mapas quanto no combate, dando uma ótima variação de gameplay. Temos uma boa variedade de armas e arcos para usar, onde eles podem ser dropados dos inimigos, achados em locais mais difíceis do mapa ou comprados na loja. Também possuímos magias simples e mecânicas clássicas de metroidvania, como pulo duplo e dash por exemplo.  A exploração é simples e objetiva, com um desafio bacana de combate de monstros e puzzles tranquilos para resolver. Tem alguns locais secretos mas são poucos, o jogo é bem direto ao ponto. O visual em pixel art é muito bonito, com destaque para alguns chefes da campanha. A trilha sonora é bem bacana também.  A história é interessante, mas nada tão profundo. Zerei e fiquei triste com a duração curta, queria mais algumas horas de diversão nele.

Having played Luna Nights before and finally having gotten to this one, I can confidently say that Team Ladybug just doesn't miss. I've made the mistake of playing this without watching the anime first, so most of the references and context were lost on me, but there was a very specific emotional core and theme to it that still hit me at the end. I'll have to catch up on the series soon, if anything this got me hugely interested.  Now, whereas Luna Nights kinda did its own thing despite obvious inspirations, Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth is a lot more blatantly inspired by Symphony of the Night than by anything else: it's apparent in the animations, the way weapons work, Deedlit even leaves afterimages behind when walking, much like Alucard, it's all pretty obvious. Even if heavily referential in that sense, this one greatly improves and adds onto what SotN did in a way that makes it a joy to play, by making combat and movement a lot more snappy and responsive (you can move while attacking, for one) and by adding some really interesting unique mechanics. At the core of the game is an element-swapping mechanic based off the two spirits you have available, each granting your attacks one type of elemental damage while also making you immune to it, which leads to really interestingly designed bossfights where you need to be swapping constantly (thankfully it only takes a button press). It get a little brain-melting with the more frantic fights, but it's a lot of fun. It's not a hard game at all, and that's fine! Exploration felt pretty great for me in this one, most of the time traversal doesn't feel like a chore the way it does in some other metroidvanias. The map and locales may not have been the most inspiring and original thing, but they did their job. One thing that felt really half-baked and baffling was the inclusion of shops; in Luna Nights they mostly made sense, but here the weapons you can buy are often really expensive and only marginally better than what you realistically have available, not to mention you're basically guaranteed to find something better for free by exploring shortly after having found one said shop. The bow felt a little unwieldy to use for a while, but I didn't take too long to get used to it, and it's still a nice option to have; there's also a handful of puzzles that require using it in somewhat creative ways, and they were pretty fun.  The graphics and music are, of course, top notch. It does feel a little silly to watch Deedlit walk basically the same way as Alucard though. One other thing I could fault the game for in this sense is that it gets somewhat cluttered visually during some bossfights: a lot of the bosses are human-sized and there are quite a few visual effects going off when you attack and do things, which leads to having trouble reading their animations and reacting accordingly if you're not taking a break from attacking to specifically do so. Thankfully these bosses work mostly through predictable patterns though, so after at most 3 tries you're bound to have the rotation figured out in a way where reading the attacks isn't even needed. Still could have been handled better.  Anyways great game, I'll watch the anime soon enough.

I picked this up after seeing endless mentions and rave reviews for it... And I honestly don't understand where it's all coming from? It feels like a half-finished M'vania from the early aughts (maybe something from SNK since the graphics are a little better than the ol' GBA fare?):   [list][*]You figure out the story in the first ~1 hour, it's not particularly subtle and basically spelled out to you... [*]...yet random anime figure you've neven seen before and have no attachment to regularly pops up to monologue "enigmatically" (you know the cliché and expressions...) at you for 30-90 seconds. I just stopped caring after a while and button-mashed to get past these gameplay interruptions. [*]There's an xp system and level-up mechanic... that doesn't really do anything, it barely even changes your stats over long time. [*]The music is very intense, in a bad way: It's more or less a constant blaring in that low-fi ~PS1 style, never fits the "spooky" style of the game and milieus and you just filter it out after a while. There's [b]no[/b] sound option to turn it off, goes without saying. [*]Of course it also has a player stun mechanic: Imagine if, say, Hollow Knight or similar games just removed the controls and made your character stand around like target dummy for ~5 seconds? Some enemies inflict this on [i]one[/i] hit, most take a little longer to build it up.[/list]   I can't say it's an outright [b]bad[/b] game but it feels like one that that released ~2005, and any qualities it might've had 20(+) years ago have long since been refined and made better by a host of other games. Only get this if you're plain [i]desperate![/i]

I've played a fair amount of Metroidvanias in my day, and this is one of the ones that gets closest to that feeling I got when I played SotN for the first time. The game play is similar, though I don't know if it's as in depth as SotN (min-maxing and such). The sound design and old school pixel art is doing a lot of the work in this aspect. I do think that people who haven't watched or read Record of Lodoss War will be a little lost when it comes to the plot, but this is a game targeted towards that demo, so I think that's okay.  Gameplay-wise, it's fairly simple. I have a gone months between playing it, putting it down, and picking it back up, and it's easy to get back into the slaying of monsters. Don't take that as a lack of interest in the story or gameplay; FFXIV is a time sink, so most of my time is spent there. But, when downtime happens, this is a great alternative while I'm waiting for the servers to come back up.  I give it an 8 out of 10. I haven't finished it, so that may change.

Story - In short this is the story of a female elf named Deedlit who mysteriously awakens in a dungeon and must discover why, how she got there and and then how to escape.   + Just like team ladybug's other projects this game has beautiful graphics as well. + A cool ikaruga style of gameplay swapping between the spirits which leads to some cool innovative boss fights. + I love how this game takes inspiration from one of my favorite metroidvanias of all time CSOTN with the beautiful animations, solid gameplay and overall feel. + Solid ending with a cliff hanger. + Boss Rush Mode. - When I crouch attack, and then immediately try to do slide the character jumps instead. - The bestiary doesn't show the name of the  weapons dropped by creatures making it difficult to collect all the weapons.
This game was gifted to me by my love because we watched the anime when we first met <3

A castlevania-style (rather than Metroid or Hollow Knight) short metroidvania (100% completion took me ~10 h) with Ikaruga-style element switching and countering. On the easier side, especially compared to the devs' previous game, Touhou Luna Nights, but it was fun enough.  Pixel art looks eyegasmic, just like with Luna Nights. Story requires at least having seen the 1990 Record of Lodoss War OVA to follow since otherwise you won't know who the hell anybody is. If you like castlevania-style metroidvanias (or want to try one as this one is fairly easy and short) it's a recommend IMO. Unless you're the kind of person who demands Hollow Knight amounts of content out of all $15-20 games, in which case wait for a sale.

A fun and simple metriodvania. The pixel art is well done and it plays well. I've never seen/read anything in this universe and I was lost with the story. But that didn't really hinder my enjoyment. It's only about 5-6 hours depending on your skill level. The game is fairly easy for the majority but does ramp up with the last couple bosses. It has a self healing and elemental switching system. But getting hit once makes it go away and you have to rebuild up your spirit levels.  Visually sometimes there is to much going on and it's difficult to follow the action. It's also dumb to put the cancel button and the spell button as the same so you'll just randomly cast spells often.  It's fairly cheap and I enjoyed it.

Pros:  Balanced Combat Great Music Nostalgic Style The story is amazingly well-crafted, and makes sense as an addition to the original canon.  Cons: Too short overall Could use more secrets to find Could use a "Phase 2" deeper in the Labyrinth, like after beating the pre-final boss challenges of the game, it feels like there should have been a second labyrinth, with more difficult monsters, and a second set of keys to collect in order to get into Nihil's chamber.

It’s a great game. It’s really interesting to see how much it actually feels like a sequel to Touhou Luna Nights because, how it feels, how it looks, how it plays, and how it sounds. And I don’t mean that negatively at all. You can really tell how much they learned from the previous game and turned that into an even better experience. Everything just feels right, and the gameplay is so smooth you just glide through the levels and have fun. Two things I still preferred in Luna Nights, were the soundtrack (which is an unfair comparison, I know) and the combat system. I really liked how Luna Nights used the time stop mechanics and how every enemy encounter was designed around it, it made the game feel quite unique. In comparison, Wonder Labyrinth feels a bit more conventional. Sure, switching elements Ikaruga style to avoid damage from certain attacks is cool, but I felt it wasn’t used enough and there was more potential there. But that’s really just a minor point that didn’t bother me in the end, because everything else is spot on. I haven’t watched the anime in years, and i think i'm going to do that again.

[b]Record of Lodoss War: Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth[/b] одразу затягує у чарівний і таємничий світ Лодосса. Висока ельфійка Дідліт прокидається в загадковому лабіринті без пам’яті, і гравець вирушає разом із нею на пошук відповідей та зустрічей з тими, хто залишив слід у її житті. Магічний світ наповнений різноманітними ворогами та босами, кожен із яких пропонує свій унікальний виклик. Важливою механікою є використання духів стихій та магії, що додає глибини боям. Пошук нових умінь дозволяє долати перешкоди та відкривати недоступні раніше шляхи. Нелінійний світ заохочує повертатися в знайомі зони, де можна відкрити нові таємниці. Анімації, деталі оточення та музика створюють відчуття живого, казкового світу, у якому хочеться залишитися. Гра не прагне вражати масштабом або експериментами, але пропонує продуману і насичену пригоду, яка однаково може захопити як новачків, так і шанувальників класичних метроідваній.

A decent little metroidvania type game, though I'll state a few caveats and pain points: - The way being dizzied happens in the game punitive in a largely annoying and uninteresting way. Plus dizzies can leave you open to a hit that will also stun you immediately again. (I don't mean being hit by thrown chains by enemies, though that's annoying as well - I have a hand injury that makes mashing directions on a d-pad physically painful. - Boss difficulty is a bit uneven. Most are very manageable, especially if you're paying attention to their patterns, and some can be cheesed in fairly direct ways (ie, just stand behind Karla, mostly). However, in some of the harder ones, there's enough visual spam on screen in a lot of the game to make it hard to 'read' and feel a little more annoying.  The game is good about having saves before bosses, but for example in the latter half almost every boss ends up being a two-stage affair, and the final boss has an even worse example of this kind of thing. So it can make it annoying if you're having trouble with the end of a boss, because you have to deal with the 'easy, boring part' ahead of it.  My completion time was ~6 hours. The worst I got stuck on a boss was probably about six attempts on the same boss or so.  Sprite quality was good. music was a mix. As someone who grew up on the old Lodoss War anime, it was neat to see a Lodoss title (last one I played was on the Dreamcast!) but the story is kinda light and most of the game is just the protagonist being confused, so it's just kinda light fan service.

I played this around a time when I was wanting to play Castlevania SotN BIGTIME (but didn't want to replay a game I knew so well) and it absolutely scratched that itch.  The game straight up feels like a mod of Castlevania SotN!  The story is a little bare-bones but it offers some interesting insight into the lore of the franchise as a whole (see below).  It's a bit short unfortunately and the archery challenge minigame is pretty tough but overall I definitely recommend the game.  PS: It's also a fantastic franchise!  Record of Lodoss War is a Japanese franchise that literally started with magazine published documentation (called replays) of an ongoing D&D campaign.  The campaign itself went on long enough that they eventually left the D&D rules and started making their own homebrew content, and the replays were so popular that the story spawned novels, anime, video games, and its own TTRPG system!  I didn't know this when I first started watching the Record of Lodoss War anime but as a D&D player when I first watched it I felt like I could literally see the dice rolling as the story went along, it was only after that my instinct was confirmed and I started digging into the franchise further.  The Record of Lodoss War franchise is probably the greatest example of a D&D group's dreams of ongoing gameplay AND making their hobby profitable that I know of, and it's one of the earliest!  I just wish more people outside of Japan knew about it.  Sure we have stuff like Critical Role and Dimension 20 and etc these days with professional actors and storytellers and comedians but this is quite literally some regular people hanging out that spawned so much win.  Even if you're not a big anime fan if you're a fan of fantasy stories and/or D&D (any version) I highly recommend digging deeper into this franchise!

awesome! given to me by my friend CheesyChip as part of our exchange and tbh i loved it! its very obviously inspired by SotN in its gameplay in the best possible way. deedlits animations are like 1-1 for Alucard but the game still feels really unique and like its own thing. the weapon shapes and whatnot are great but i love the bow, and i absolutely ADORE the switching between fire/wind thing in combat for certain attacks. i think their passive buffs are a little whatever but boss fights are where this game's combat shines. from the very first one theyre super engaging and genuinely difficult but always fair at the end. it's super short and i was both wanting more and also happy to put a lid on it. i dont know anything about lodoss war and the storyline being like, oh she's dreaming bc its about grief was like, fine but just not something i cared for particularly for without knowing who the characters were. i also thought the map design was good but to be honest it lacked in personality. it's just kind of a space and feels more like levels than a true metroidvania, as once youre done with a "stage" as the game calls them you never need to return, and unlocking the shortcuts back doesnt really matter. but it is good! i'd like to see a bigger version of this one day, maybe with a vampire in it....

Record of Lodoss War - Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth é uma surpresa extremamente agradável. A jogabilidade é fluida, responsiva e prazerosa — cada movimento parece afinado com cuidado, e a mecânica de troca de elementos traz uma camada estratégica que mantém o combate sempre interessante. Como metroidvania, o jogo brilha: exploração divertida, progressão bem dosada e mapas que recompensam a curiosidade. Uma experiência curta, elegante e memorável. Recomendo com entusiasmo.

The art style is amazing, but this is the only good point about this game.  There are basically 3 types of weapons. The bow, a meele that hits forward, and a melee that hits in arc. Besides the strenght of the weapon, there no other attributes worth mentioning. Weapons are the only thing you can equip.  The element spirit switch is interesting, but it feels incomplete, considering there are only two elements you can have.   Dodging is a mess. The dodge button only go a short distance backwards, it is not possible to go forward. The only option to actually dodge enemies and projectiles is jumping, or swtiching to the correct element if the projectile is fire or wind, anything else is impossible to defend and it will cause a lot of damage.  Enemies are bland, boring, and more or less a variation of the same thing. Bosses are too easy to kill, and you are either almost invulnerable or you have no defense at all.  Exploring is just walking around opening colored doors.  I imagine the story may make sense you have the context, which is not my case.

First of all I really have to address the SOTN comparisons which I just don't get outside of it being a metroidvania... okay so it's got combat, and move progression and platforming... wow it must be SOTN. This comparison honestly does both games a disservice, for one there's a kind of ikaruga style elemental system at play here that changes up certain key fundamentals. Secondly this game in particular is exceptionally linear in its progression and puzzles and mysteries are sporadic at best.   Hell even the aesthetics are vastly different.   Combat here also feels more defined and as long as you're not just spamming magic blindly you'll have a good time with it.   So it's NOT a SOTN successor, but is it good? Yes, aside for the linear progression the aesthetics are great, bosses are fun and bombastic though their mechanics weren't always intuitive to me. Most were and figuring them out on the fly felt satisfying. Music is pretty barebones if I'm honest and aside from a few atmospheric tracks it mostly felt just kinda there.   Short, sweet, fairly inventive, good but not great metroidvania. Enjoy.

Приятная метроидвания с приятным визуальным 🖼️ и неплохим музыкальным 🎵 сопровождением. Играется довольно легко, но оно и не плохо. Удобная карта и парочка боссов, которые могут заставить попотеть на несколько попыток (если, конечно, сражаться без зелий лечения 🩹). Проходиться игра быстро, но вполне может скрасить вечер, к тому же вполне приятное дополнение к основному произведению.

Доволі непогана метроїдванія. Не затягнута, зрозуміла, кімнати мають помірну складність, є цікава механіка перемикання між стихіями, і доволі багато босів, як для такої компактної гри. Причому боси, хоч і складні, але не такі хардкорні, як у Hollow Knight. Вони цілком доступні для тих, хто колись грав у подібні ігри, але тепер не має багато часу відпрацьовувати патерни.  Однак це повністю шаблонна метроїдванія, хоч і з оригінальною механікою стихій. Тут є абсолютно стандартні вміння - подвійний стрибок, який дозволяє дістатися до високих платформ, підкат, що дає змогу пролізти у низькі вузькі проходи, і дихання під водою. Сюжет і всесвіт мені не близькі, бо аніме, за яким створено гру, я не дивився. Тож можу рекомендувати її лише тим, хто хоче чогось у стилі Castlevania, але без надмірної складності чи оригінальності. Бо грається вона загалом приємно, хоч і не захоплює.

[h2]훌륭한 아트와 레벨 디자인을 지닌 악마성 라이크 메트로배니아[/h2]   [list] [*][h3]평가: A (호)[/h3] [*]출시/플레이: 21년 / 25년 5월 [*]조작/언어: 키보드/한국어 [*]난이도 옵션: 없음, 소비템 x [*]도전과제: 15/25 [/list]   일본 판타지 소설 로도스도 전기의 30주년 기념 2차 창작 게임.  캐릭터 모션, 다양한 무기, RPG식 성장요소 등 다양한 면에서 캐슬바니아가 떠올랐다. 특히 백대쉬는 다른 게임에서 처음 봄ㅋㅋㅋ 이걸 대체 어떻게 쓰냐?  아무튼 개인적으로 캐슬바니아 시리즈 정말 안 맞는 편인데, 이 게임은 오히려 재밌게 플레이했다. 개인적으로 Ladybug 작품들 중 가장 마음에 들었음.    일단 도트와 모션 그래픽, 연출이 너무나 훌륭했다. 개인적으로 아트도 게임성이라고 믿는데, 이 게임은 떼깔이 너무 좋았음. 특히 컷씬 역할을 하는 연출에 공을 많이 들인 게 보였다. 개발진이 로도스도 찐팬인 듯.  원작을 조금이라도 알았더라면 게임 연출에 진짜 몰입했을 거 같은데 그러지 못한 게 아쉽다. 서사와 메시지가 중요한 게임인데, 일반적인 팬 게임 마냥 배경 설명이나 인물 소개 없이 무작정 먹이더라. 스토리텔링에 조금 더 신경 써줬으면 정말 좋았을 거 같음.    한편 Ladybug 게임에서는 매번 분절적인 레벨 디자인이 아쉬웠는데, 이 게임은 꽤나 만족스러웠다. 맵간 유기성이 한껏 개선됐고, 덕분에 포털 개수가 크게 늘지 않았음에도 탐험 편의성이 크게 올라갔다. 플랫포밍 관련 어빌리티가 많아서 백트래킹이 비교적 시원시원한 것도 좋았음. 수집요소도 딱 적절했고.    전투는 오랜만에 근접 전투를 채용했는데 개인적으로 취향에 맞았다. 전투 시스템은 정령 원소와 정령 3레벨 회복이 특징적이었는데, 각각 SYNCHRONICITY PROLOGUE의 속성과 동방 루나 나이츠의 그레이즈 회복과 유사했음. 하지만 SYNCHRONICITY PROLOGUE보다 부유 컨트롤이 편리하고, 루나 나이츠 보다 회복이 자유로운 등 편의성 면에선 훨씬 발전된 게 눈에 띄었다.    다만 보스 전투는 여전히 아쉬웠음. 보스 패턴을 파훼하기에는 너무 까다롭고, [spoiler]평타보다 마법이 너무 세서 굳이 평타로 무리해서 딜하느니 적당히 정령으로 MP 흡수하고 HP 회복하면서 마법 난사하는 게 만능이었음. 기믹 보스에 가깝게 느껴질 정도.[/spoiler] 이쯤되면 게임사에서 추구하는 전투 방향성이 이런 건가 싶기도 하고... 개인적으로는 머리 박는 보스전을 더 좋아하는 편이라 아쉬웠다.    그럼에도 전체적으로 정말 만족스러운 메트로배니아였다. [h3]로도스도 팬이거나, 캐슬바니아, 고해상도 도트를 좋아하는 사람에게 특히 추천.[/h3]  원작 요약 유튜브 같은 거부터 보고 게임하면 더 즐길 수 있을 듯.

[i]Tem jogos que a gente joga. E tem outros que a gente sente. Record of Lodoss War -Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth- é desses segundos. Ele não quer reinventar o gênero  quer celebrar tudo o que fez os Metroidvanias serem mágicos, com o toque nostálgico e poético que só uma aventura estrelada por Deedlit, a elfa mais icônica de Lodoss, poderia oferecer...   Gameplay: o equilíbrio entre leveza e letalidade  Sabe aquele tipo de jogo que parece uma sinfonia entre movimento e precisão? É isso aqui. A Deedlit se move com uma fluidez que lembra os tempos de Castlevania: Symphony of the Night  e não é coincidência. O jogo abraça descaradamente essa inspiração, mas com o seu próprio charme. O sistema de espíritos elementais (Sylph e Salamander) é um toque genial: alternar entre vento e fogo não é só estética, é mecânica de sobrevivência. Cada troca muda a dinâmica do combate e do movimento  flutuar, desviar, contra-atacar. É intuitivo, viciante e traz um ritmo quase musical às lutas.  A progressão é limpa e satisfatória: ganha-se novas armas, habilidades e magias num fluxo natural, sem te afogar em menus ou grind. É aquele tipo de jogo que te faz pensar: “só mais um mapa…”, e quando vê, já são 3 da manhã.   Gráficos: pixel art feita com alma  Se pixel art é uma arte, Wonder Labyrinth é uma pintura viva. Cada frame, cada sprite, cada reflexo de luz carrega um capricho absurdo. Os efeitos de magia são um espetáculo à parte especialmente quando o fogo e o vento se misturam em um balé visual que te faz esquecer que está jogando um título 2D.  A ambientação mistura melancolia e beleza. Há algo de solitário e encantado em cada sala  como se Deedlit estivesse andando entre memórias desbotadas, tentando lembrar quem ela era e por que tudo parece tão distante.  Trilha sonora: sussurros de uma lembrança antiga  A música é suave, elegante e cheia de alma. Ela não tenta dominar sua atenção  ela te acompanha, te embala, e quando você percebe, já está totalmente imerso naquele clima de sonho nostálgico. Os efeitos sonoros são delicados, e cada golpe, passo e feitiço têm um som que parece pensado para transmitir leveza  algo raro num jogo de ação.   Narrativa: entre memórias e reflexos  A história é mais simbólica do que direta. Não espere diálogos longos ou longas cutscenes  o jogo é mais sobre emoção e lembrança do que sobre enredo linear. É como se Deedlit estivesse vagando num limbo de memórias, revivendo o que perdeu e tentando encontrar um sentido em meio à solidão. Para os fãs do anime ou das novels, há um peso extra  é quase uma despedida silenciosa dessa personagem lendária.   Game Feel e experiência do jogador  Do ponto de vista de jogabilidade, o “feel” de Wonder Labyrinth é quase terapêutico. O controle é preciso, a resposta é instantânea, e o movimento de Deedlit é um dos mais suaves que já vi num jogo 2D. Você sente o vento quando flutua. Sente o impacto da lâmina. E sente o silêncio quando tudo para. É um jogo que te coloca em um estado de fluxo, onde cada ação parece natural  como se o controle fosse uma extensão da sua vontade.   Comparação e legado  Se Symphony of the Night é o rei, Wonder Labyrinth é o príncipe que honra seu legado com graça. Não tenta competir  homenageia. E, no meio de tantas tentativas modernas de reinventar o gênero, é refrescante ver um jogo que simplesmente diz:  “Lembra por que você se apaixonou por isso tudo?”   Veredito Final   [list]     [*]Gameplay: Suave, responsivo e mágico   [*]Gráficos: Pixel art de encher os olhos   [*]Trilha Sonora: Atmosférica e emocional   [*]História: Poética, introspectiva e sutil   [*]Inovação: Simplicidade elevada à perfeição   [code] “Entre luz e sombra, a memória de Lodoss ainda vive.”

It's okay... but that's it. The element switching mechanic is fun but outside of that it plays it too safe. It has a few glaring issues that somehow got through the final version, nothing gamebreaking but really annoying and it could have been easily fixed. First, the Cancel button and the use spell button are the same. Normally this wouldn't be a problem, a lot of games do this, however, for some reason in this game whenever you close the pause menu with the cancel button it immediately uses a spell right as the game unpauses, wasting mana. It's also very hard to figure out which element you are in, and this is a glaring issue since the entire game revolves around this mechanic. The only way to tell which element you are using is to either look for the tiny familiar flying somewhere near you or look at the orb at the very top of the HUD. None of these options is good while you are in the middle of action and only have a split second to decide. Overall, the game is not bad but there's not much going on for it. It's fun, but the entire thing is over in like 4 hours, so if you pick it do it on sale. I however, recommend picking up the newest metroidvania title by the same devs instead: Bladechimera. It's miles better in my opinion.

[h1]Not What I Was Expecting[/h1]  To best describe this it's definitely not what I had expected at all! The story is deep and rich. The plot movement is actually good! Though I have to say Boss Rush mode is not for someone short on patience unless you have time to memorize all the boss mechanics from all the fights. Record of Lodoss War: Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth es uno de esos metroidvanias que evoca nostalgia y te mantiene pegado a la pantalla, incluso si no conoces nada del anime original. Y eso ya es un mérito, porque aunque sea una secuela del universo de Lodoss, la historia es lo suficientemente sencilla como para disfrutarla sin haber visto ni un episodio. Dicho esto, si eres fan de la franquicia, vas a sonreír en varias escenas: los guiños y la personalidad de los personajes suman un plus que se agradece, y todo ello hace que el mundo de Lodoss se sienta más vivo y presente que nunca.   Tampoco podemos olvidar que el desarrollo del juego contó con la supervisión directa de Ryō Mizuno, el creador de la obra original, lo que garantiza que los personajes, la ambientación y la historia respetan el espíritu auténtico de Lodoss y se sientan fieles al universo que tantos conocemos.  El homenaje a Castlevania: Symphony of the Night es evidente y, francamente, una delicia. Cada animación, cada salto y cada ataque recuerdan al clásico de Toru Hagihara y Koji Igarashi, pero sin llegar a sentirse una copia descarada, el juego tiene su propia identidad y lo demuestra constantemente.   Deedlit responde de manera ágil y precisa, y el sistema de espíritus (cambiar entre fuego y viento) le añade un pequeño giro estratégico a los combates y a ciertos obstáculos del mapa, sin complicar demasiado la experiencia. A veces la dificultad brilla por su ausencia, especialmente con enemigos normales, pero los jefes tienen momentos donde sí necesitas pensar y dominar tus habilidades, aunque nada que llegue a frustrar de verdad.  Cada zona está cuidada, el mapa es lo bastante intuitivo como para no perderse, y los secretos son justitos pero muy satisfactorios de descubrir. Es un metroidvania que va al grano, sin relleno innecesario, y eso hace que cada minuto invertido se sienta valioso.  Visualmente es una gozada, el pixelart es delicado y detallado, con escenarios que parecen vivos, y la música acompaña maravillosamente, creando atmósferas que te meten de lleno en el laberinto. No es que sea la banda sonora más épica que escucharás en tu vida, pero cumple y aporta emoción en cada momento.  Si tuviera que ponerle algún “pero”, sería la linealidad y la simplicidad de algunos enemigos, se nota que el juego no busca desafiarte constantemente, sino ofrecer una experiencia ágil, disfrutable y honesta. Y eso, en un mundo saturado de metroidvanias interminables, se agradece.  En resumen, Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth es un pequeño tesoro para los amantes del género. Un homenaje brutal a Symphony of the Night y con suficiente encanto para enamorar tanto a veteranos de Lodoss como a recién llegados. No va a cambiar tu vida, pero las horas que pases en su laberinto te van a dejar con una sonrisa y la sensación de haber jugado algo sincero y con corazón.

Wer in den 90ern gelebt hat und damals schon ein Nerd war, erinnert sich vielleicht daran, wie schwer es war, an Animes zu kommen. Heute ist die deutsche Medienwelt damit überschwemmt, aber das war nicht immer so.  Es gab Biene Maja und Wickie und Heidi, von denen die meisten nicht wussten, dass sie aus Japan kamen, sowie Akira für den damals spezielleren Geschmack. Aber das war es auch schon fast. Fast.  Denn es gab auch die Anime-Nächte auf Vox. Die ganze Nacht lang liefen Anime-Serien wie Neon Genesis Evangelion, Gunsmith Cats und Blue Submarine No. 6 auf Japanisch mit deutschen Untertiteln.   Und da lief auch [b]Record of Lodoss War[/b]. Dabei handelt es um eine ziemlich klassische Fantasy-Serie, die vor allem durch ihren Oldschool-Charme besticht. Drachen, böse Zauberer, Elfen, Zwerge, Diebe, Goblins und so.  Im Mittelpunkt steht Parn, ein junger Held, mit seiner Gruppe aus klassischen Rollenspiel-Charakteren.  Einer dieser Charaktere ist die Elfe Deedlit, im japanischen Original Deedolito oder einfach Deedo genannt.  Sie spielen wir in Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth.   Das Spiel ist ein solides Metroidvania. 2D-Platformer mit RPG-Elementen, einigen Waffen, Schwertern, Bögen, Tränken, Magie, einigen Bossen und genügend Speicherpunkten. Außerdem schöne Pixelart und passende Musikuntermalung.  Eine Besonderheit gibt es: Wir haben zwei Geister, die uns begleiten und zwischen denen wir wechseln können: Sylph (Luft) und Salamander (Feuer).  Die Wahl des Geistes beeinflusst, wovon wir verletzt werden können und wie viel Schaden wir bestimmten Gegnern zufügen. Und sie geben uns ein paar Spezialfähigkeiten.   Insgesamt ist das Spiel eine recht spaßige Angelegenheit.  Wenn das Genre etwas für Dich ist und Du Record of Lodoss War vielleicht sogar noch von früher kennst, kannst Du hier bedenkenlos zugreifen. Du wirst nicht enttäuscht.   c_Alien für [url=https://steamcommunity.com/groups/FieldysShorts]Fieldy's Shorts [/url]

As I adore Symphony of the Night, I've craved for a game that captures its core essence. This seemed to be it, but deviates due to its gimmicky mechanics. Still, one of the best modern Metroidvanias I've played.  Like many modern metroidvanias it had to use some gimmicks: The "Polarity" spirit system similar to Ikaruga or Outland. The protagonist, Deedlit, can switch between a Fire Spirit and a Wind Spirit. These spirits function like familiars, and also define the elemental type of her attacks, spells, special abilities and the types of enemy attacks she can nullify or absorb. The target-able bow is more than a weapon, it's also a puzzle mechanic with its ricocheting arrows. Mastery of holding jump button (with the wind spirit ability to "float") while aiming and shooting the bow seems to be key to success on some points and battles.  Since I've played Ender Lilies six months ago (a fantastic experience!), I've played Oris, Touhou LN and Blasphemous without having as great of a blast as from this game on a modern Metroidvania!

Об этой игре узнал совершенно случайно и прошел сразу же после Hollow Knight. Хочу сказать, что это отличная метроидвания в духе Симфонии ночи. Здесь нет проклятых соулс механик и сложность вполне адекватная. Это как раз та игра, в которую можно сыграть после учебы/работы, чтобы расслабиться и получить удовольствие. Игра основана на одноименной серии новелл и аниме. Знание первоисточника приветствуется, но не обязательно. Это совершенно отдельная история, которая никак не связана с основным сюжетом. Графика и анимации выполнены на высшем уровне. Жаль, что нет полноценного пиксель арта в плане заставок или портретов. Даже как-то обидно, что такая классная игра не на слуху. Это отличный пример того, что в метроидваниях не должно быть зубодробильной сложности для того, чтобы получать, от нее, удовольствие. Очень рекомендую!

There's a decently complex element and weapon type system, but the game is easy enough that you can largely ignore these. Like Luna Nights, it's a metroidvania but extremely linear. A good, solid game.  Not perfect, but fun and plays well.  The good: - Graphics - great use of retro style graphics and they are very evocative - Controls - Deedlit is very responsive and agile and you have many options for dealing with challenges - Boss Fights - the highlight of the game!  Each boss is distinctive and interesting, and the later ones can be challenging (if you don't spam healing potions like a wimp!) - Story - it ties in well with the source material (the "Record of Lodoss War" short stories, mangas, and animes) - Deedlit - Deedlit accurately reflects her source material; she's a glass-cannon who can dish out a lot of damage and is highly agile and capable, but needs to be careful about taking damage as she's fragile  The bad: - Enemies - regular enemies just aren't generally much of a challenge as Deedlit's attacks are quite powerful and they take some time to react to her and attack, and once you learn their attacks they're easy to deal with - Gameplay - while the game is set in a labyrinth, the gameplay is generally linear, with Deedlit just crossing back and forth over the same terrain; you don't have many actual options or any opportunities to actually get lost  Summary: - A good game with an excellent foundation, but I feel that some potential with the game is left on the table... if the game had branching paths and more challenging enemies, it would have been excellent.  Worth buying!

Quando comprei esse jogo, eu não sabia exatamente o que esperar. O trailer não me chamou tanta atenção em termos de jogabilidade, mas a estética me conquistou, a pixel art é lindíssima. Eu também estava numa fase de jogar metroidvanias, e acabei comprando principalmente porque meu ex-namorado tinha colocado o jogo na lista de desejos. Ele costumava ter bom gosto nesse tipo de jogo, então confiei que haveria um motivo por trás da escolha.  E realmente, o jogo entrega uma boa experiência. Ele tem uma estrutura mais linear do que metroidvanias clássicos, com menos liberdade em alguns momentos, e a variedade visual poderia ser maior, já que algumas áreas acabam repetitivas. Além disso, é relativamente curto: dá para terminar e explorar tudo em cerca de 5 a 8 horas.  Apesar disso, ele tem muito valor. Os controles são sólidos e responsivos, a dificuldade aparece conforme você avança (não é tudo tão óbvio), e a narrativa é envolvente, com momentos realmente emocionantes. Infelizmente, meu controle quebrou e não cheguei a platinar, mas ainda assim valeu cada hora que joguei.  No geral, é um metroidvania curto, bonito e marcante, que consegue se destacar mais pela atmosfera e pela história do que pela exploração.

What an excellent little castlevania game. Short, to the point, and along the way it constantly gives plenty of new items to the player to play around with (magic, weapons, abilities - normal stuff). Movement is snappy, special movement options are easy to execute, and on top: Deedlith moves very smoothly, with almost Alucard-like hand shuffle,  It's not too hard, some skill is needed to beat it, but main "thing" of this game is 2 elementals that help Deedlith, that you can swap between. They add their own element to attacks (some enemies are susceptible to only one), and also they absorb same element attacks replenishing the mana pool.  The game is somewhat related to anime predecesor, no knowledge of Lodoss War backstory is needed. Characters and motivations are shortly explained, so newcomers won't feel lost.  Music is amazing (often hits Tekken levels of hype), animation tip top, highly recommend

This is a relatively short and easy Metroidvania as long as you understand and master the element switching. If so, the first half of the game is very easy, even some of the bosses where you can negate most of the damage. The second half of the game brings some more traps, platforming and enemies that can tank some damage but it is still only a moderate difficulty.  It's the Castlevania style of the genre, you get levels, an HP bar, stats and items. So you're making it as easy or difficult as you want with the amount of time you spent gaining levels, weapons you decide to use and if you want to uses spells enemies and bosses are weak to or not.  It's fun to move around with Deedlit, a good amount of upgrades, almost all related to your two elements. Either making you stronger in battle or easier traversal. Nothing really game breaking either, it's all small upgrades to what you already have. There is a nice flow to it, switching midair for traversal or mid battle to protect yourself.  Secrets are relatively obvious, you don't have to hit every walls to see if they break, as long as you look closely you can see the cracks. Teleportation room make backtracking relatively easy and only a few rooms locked you have to go back to later. A door color system visible on the map make it easy to know where to go back to so you don't have to check everywhere each time.  It took me about 6 hours to finish the game and maybe another or two to get all the achievements. Without looking at a guide or some hints there were one enemy and one secret rooms I could not find. Also one weapon with a very specific way to get, with the in-game beastiary you can see where to get it but not how. The hardest achievement for me was related to an archery minigame where getting A Rank was easy but S required almost perfect performance.
An absolute blast, especially if Castlevania: Symphony of the Night or other Metroid-Vania's are your thing.

I am a huge fan of metroidvanias. I grew up on some of the original inspirations for Symphony of the Night: Metroid, Super Metroid, Castlevania II, Ys 3 (Yes, I know, it isn't super exploratory, it has super lite elements of it)...So I'm always on the lookout for a new game in that style. Enter in Deedlit. I played it back on GamePass and, at the time of this writing, GhostLP is doing a play through (DM Darius did a LP a few years back). So I decided to formally get my own copy and play through it. I'm glad that I did.  For those who know, this takes place in the Record of Lodoss War world / anime. So some familiarity is nice to have but not fully needed to get the most out of the game. For those of us who have not seen the anime, all you need to know is that Deedlit has found herself in a mysterious labyrinth chasing after her lover while her friends caution her about doing so. What follows is a rather enjoyable romp done in the metroidvania style.  So much of the reason for it is because everything moves so flipping smoothly! Deedlit is always on the move whether she's platforming or slicing monsters in half. She's responsive and does what is desired of her. She has access to spells to help defeat opponents and the majority of them are well hidden behind secret walls or out of the way locations. The map feels fairly natural as it goes about the adventure. One of the things that sets this apart from other games is the wind/fire mechanic. By switching her alignment with the 2 spirits, Deedlit is able to damage her enemies with it as well as absorb those attacks. This in turn restores her MP. If she increases her alignment with them by defeating / damaging enemies, that particular spirit will restore her health when maxed out. This is important when exploring and fighting bosses as it helps keep her health at a reasonable level (if not maxed out).  Graphically, the game looks very nice. There's no confusion about where there's stable platforms. There's the woods, an overgrown ruin complete with killer plants that fire needles, a volcano with lava that is flowing both on the ground and in the background    The music is alright and enjoyable. I don't remember much from it. The sound effects all make sense and have some heft to them.  Wonder Labyrinth is a great game. Even if you don't know the anime. It scratches that metroidvania itch that I've been having for awhile. It's an even easier pill to swallow if you can pick it up on sale. I admit that I would like a little bit more game, however it's so tightly woven that it's hard to argue with the experience. Short games done well is such a difficult task that it is impressive. Team Ladybug should be proud of themselves and I can easily imagine this being a lot of folks' Symphony of the Night.

Record of Lodoss War: Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth is a beautifully crafted Metroidvania that invites players into the rich fantasy world of the classic Lodoss War series. Developed by Team Ladybug and WSS Playground, and published by WSS Playground and PLAYISM, the game centers on Deedlit, a skilled elven warrior who finds herself trapped in a mysterious labyrinth filled with danger and secrets. The narrative, while closely tied to the Lodoss War lore, offers enough intrigue to engage both newcomers and longtime fans alike, although familiarity with the source material certainly deepens the experience.  The gameplay follows many conventions beloved by Metroidvania enthusiasts: exploration of interconnected areas, gradual acquisition of new abilities, and a steady unlocking of previously inaccessible paths. What sets this game apart is its innovative elemental mechanic, which allows Deedlit to harness the powers of two spirits—Wind and Fire. Each spirit offers unique abilities that affect both combat and environmental puzzles, compelling players to think strategically about when and how to switch between elements. This adds a refreshing layer of depth to the otherwise familiar platforming and action mechanics, encouraging thoughtful engagement rather than simple button-mashing.  Visually, Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth is a standout. The pixel art is meticulously detailed, capturing the ethereal beauty of its fantasy setting with vibrant colors and smooth animations. From lush forest glades to shadowy dungeon corridors, the environments feel alive and carefully designed, evoking a sense of wonder that complements the game’s title. The character sprites move fluidly, and the enemy designs range from whimsical to menacing, contributing to a varied and immersive atmosphere. Accompanying the visuals is a stirring orchestral soundtrack that heightens the emotional impact of exploration and combat, reinforcing the epic fantasy tone without ever overwhelming the gameplay.  Combat in the game is accessible but satisfying. Deedlit’s moveset is versatile thanks to the elemental spirits, which grant her distinct offensive and defensive capabilities. Enemies present a steady challenge, with boss fights standing out as particularly memorable encounters that require players to observe attack patterns and react accordingly. While some players might find the difficulty leaning towards the easier side, especially compared to other games in the genre, the balance allows for a smooth progression that doesn’t frustrate newcomers. The puzzles, although not overly complex, cleverly integrate the elemental mechanics, rewarding players who experiment and think critically.  One aspect where the game could be seen as lacking is in its map design and narrative clarity. The labyrinth’s layout is relatively straightforward compared to other sprawling Metroidvanias, which may disappoint those looking for intricate maze-like exploration. Additionally, the story is delivered through dialogue and cutscenes that often presume familiarity with the Lodoss War universe. For players new to the franchise, some plot points may feel confusing or underdeveloped, though the core mystery and character motivations remain engaging enough to drive the experience forward.  Overall, Record of Lodoss War: Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth successfully blends classic Metroidvania gameplay with fresh mechanics and a visually striking world. Its elemental spirit system adds meaningful complexity, while the art and music elevate the game into a memorable fantasy adventure. Though its length is moderate, offering around eight hours of gameplay for thorough explorers, it manages to provide a satisfying balance of challenge, charm, and narrative depth. Whether you’re a fan of the Lodoss War saga or simply enjoy well-made Metroidvanias with a strong aesthetic and innovative gameplay, this title is well worth diving into.  Rating: 9/10

Record of Lodoss War Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth- 100% Completion Review Score: 10/10  Record Of Lodoss War Deedlit In Wonder Labyrinth Is A Metroidvania Game Set In A Fantasy World Where You Play As A Female Elf Hero As She Attempts To Get Out Of A Dungeon Of Which She Has No Memory Of Entering, After Fully Completing This Game I'll Give Some Pro's, Neutrals and Cons   PRO'S: -Good protagonist who is well written, stylish and cute -Good sprite animations and effects -Good platforming and movement with a well designed map -Good environment details that are very detailed -Good story, a bit predictable but still has plenty of twists and turns -Good x-appeal, doesn't shy away from the spice -Good amount of secrets to find -Good amount of puzzles that aren't too easy or too hard -Good combat that allows you to use multiple types of weapons, magic and spirits -Good difficulty balance, easy at the start, ramps up at mid and a bit tough but still fair at the end -Good enemy design -Has a Boss Rush mode after you complete the game if your into that type of thing -Apparently has books and anime of the same name? I didn't know this prior to playing this game but interesting to note, i suppose this game takes place long after those stories -Good performance, no lag or bugs  NEUTRAL'S: "No Score Change From These" -Game length about 6+ hours -Some of the enemies near the end of the game have a bit too much hp but not that big of a deal  CON'S: -Nothing i can think of  Overall Record of Lodoss War Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth was quite a good Metroidvania game, it's incredibly stylish, has solid animations and honestly was fun to play from start to finish, it's a bit short at just 6+ hours but it's a good enough game that it really doesn't matter, if you like Metroidvania games then this is definitely one worth considering but as always, It's.. Up.. To.. You, and i'll leave it at that.  (^_−).
