A Metroidvania based on Record of Lodoss War / Rōdosu-tō Senki series, no prior knowledge of the series is needed to enjoy the game since there is very little story in it.  Game is short, fast-paced and fun. You unlock abilities (like hover and bows) very early which usually take forever to unlock in other Metroidvanias. And yes, you can cast magic and shoot your bow while hovering in style. Spirit of wind and fire mechanic is nice, it reminds me of Castlevania Lord of Shadows red/blue but done much better. Thrown items like boomerang are pure joy, no need to wait for them to return to the user, just spam them.  Difficulty wise, game is relatively easy since you unlock lots of skills to choose from, like me and Sanji's favorite, the pure awesome skill of invisibility. Nothing beats watching the enemy hitting thin air. If you play with handicap or attempt to obtain the achievements, the game is much harder however.  Visually the game is nice to look at, and i love looking at Deedlit's afterimage all over the place. But the background sprites could be done better or varied a little bit to make it more interesting.  Difficulty: 4/10 Fun: Spamming Boomerangs, Magic and Bow while hovering / 10 Steam Deck: Works, crashed once, but lots of checkpoints anyway. Keyboard/Joystick: This is probably better on keyboard since you have more control and precision.

游戏内容比较有趣，玩家可以使用风火两种形态来免疫BOSS的风火属性攻击，而且对应属性升到3级可以给非本属性回血，运用得当的话相当于无限血瓶，具有一定的策略性，不过就内容量和质量来看70元是不太值的。  让我玩得不舒服的是后期的一些机关，我不知道有没有高人是用正统方法过的，反正我是卡无敌帧硬蹭蹭过的，十分恶心。 比如中期有个两连齿轮车，你需要在右边坐上车后射击齿轮上半使吊车向左移动，然后射击齿轮旁边的齿轮让左边的吊车过来，但这个操作我用手柄根本做不到，使用摇杆很容易就偏了，偏了车子就往回走，磨了半天吊车过去了射另一个齿轮不见对面吊车过来，然后自己坐的吊车开始后退，你再用弓射齿轮摇杆会大幅偏移，结果导致你回到初始位置。 最后我放弃了叫另一辆吊车，而是在第一个吊车过去后直接跳到刺上用滑铲硬生生划过，结果过了这关我到了下一层又看见了同款吊车，顿时无语了。不过还好，这次不用叫第二辆吊车，稍微磨一磨还是可以可以过的。

Buen juego, pero el GRAN PROBLEMA que tiene es la pésima elección de colores para el personaje principal. El juego se basa en el concepto del Ikaruga, en el que según qué color sea el espíritu que controles (AZUL-ROJO) así responderá tu ataque/defensa. Es una idea genial y muy buena, el ENORME PROBLEMA es que este un juego de acción en le que has de tomar decisiones muy rápidas para intercambiar los colores, especialmente contra los JEFES FINALES, y NO HAY manera de saber cual es el color de tu espíritu, excepto APARTANDO LA VISTA DE LA ACCIÓN para mirar arriba, donde está la esfera. Tu personaje también cambia de color, PERO ES PRÁCTICAMENTE INAPRECIABLE y es una puñetera tortura tener que estar apartando la mirada para ver qué color estas controlando, reduciendo drásticamente la jugabilidad. ¿Tanto trabajo costaba remarcar de alguna manera mucho más evidente el color que manejas?

Recommended IF on sales.  I never review games but i do want to say fews word about this game:  On steam, the review score is "very positive", which pushed me to buy the game. After playing it, I can say the gameplay is very good but the game as a whole suffers from 2 problems:  - the overall story and character are blank except if you are Lodoss War fan and enjoyer. If you do not know the character or background, then nothing will really allow you to relate to them and be interested. You will just explore - beat the boss - repeat as if you were playing a random castlevania clone.   - the game is rather easy and way too short (only 12h to close it, like at 100%, except the bow challenge and some enemy dropped weapon). Dont put more than 10 euros on it.  On a very positive note, on the gameplay side, the air/fire ability that you unlock early in the game with the possibility to absorb enemy attack using the corresponding element is very interesting. Also, the secondary arc weapon is nicely implemented.  Conclusion: the game has been made as a cheap way to make money, or/and, as a tribute to SOTN and Lodoss Chronicles (those musics feel so SOTN indeed).  It is really made for the fans but it feels cheap to me.  I somehow enjoyed it but I dont think i would recommend it at full price because it simply too short and too easy even if the gameplay is indeed very good.

不会做类银可以不做，一趟玩下来和线性关卡完全没什么区别，4小时全收集通关的流程也能卖原价68？武器套皮改数值几乎没什么差异化，角色成长空间也少得可怜。地图设计更是勾石中的勾石，每个房间放几个对称阶梯堆几个对称小怪；中期获得的能力在跑图上用不了多少次；一个颜色的门解锁了结果发现大部分都是单纯连接两个已经到达过的房间，剩下的用来连下一章的房间，有半点类银地图设计的诚意吗？有的房间顶头放一堆很难够到的厚皮小怪，配合垃圾进门小怪刷新机制和垃圾三秒控制debuff机制，属于是纯纯恶心人。 弓箭机关这个点如果制作组愿意再多加挖掘，放一些更加精密的设计进去的话可能会给人留下更深的印象，但玩一趟下来给人的印象是为了存在而存在。 剧情可以说完全没有。 音乐，美术，操作手感都不错，这也算是Ladybug一向的优点，但总体看来这款游戏对于对类银元素有期待的玩家以及想要性价比的玩家来说完全不值得入手。

Record of Lodoss War-Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth- is a visually stunning game with beautiful animation and music that perfectly capture the fantasy atmosphere of the Lodoss War universe. The boss fights are a definite highlight, with some engaging encounters that showcase the game's strengths. However, despite these positives, it's difficult to wholeheartedly recommend the game due to several glaring issues.  One of the biggest drawbacks is the bow mechanic. Unlike the fluid combat, using the bow requires manual aiming and is primarily utilized to shoot gears to open doors throughout the game. This mechanic disrupts the overall gameplay flow and feels more like an unnecessary chore rather than an engaging feature. The frequent need to stop and aim takes away from the game's pacing and makes exploration feel tedious.  Another major issue is the game's inconsistent difficulty. Some bosses can be effortlessly defeated, while others present an unexpectedly steep challenge with little warning. This imbalance can make progression feel erratic and frustrating, reducing the sense of accomplishment that should come with overcoming a consistently upward challenge.  On the brighter side, the ability to fast-forward dialogue is a welcome addition, allowing players to control the pace of the narrative without unnecessary interruptions. The inclusion of multiple weapon types with distinct attack patterns is also appreciated, although the differences between them feel somewhat minor, leading to a lack of true variety in combat.  Overall, while Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth has some redeeming qualities, such as its presentation and occasional enjoyable boss fights. The frustrating bow mechanics and uneven difficulty prevent it from reaching its full potential. It's worth considering for fans of the series or those who can overlook its flaws, but others might find the experience more frustrating than rewarding.

The 2nd game from Team Ladybug, after Touhou Luna Nights.  Compared to Luna Nights, this one has a few major quality of life improvements, such as more warp rooms and a small radar screen at the bottom. It is also longer, roughly 5-6 hours to beat on first pass.  Gameplay wise, the dual element switch system results in a few neat Ikaruga moments where you have to switch polarity to dodge attacks, and the boss fights are quite a bit harder than Luna Nights.  Two major disappointments 1. Lack of equipment variety. There are a ton of melee weapons and bows, but due to the game mechanic, you're always going to use the fastest option.  2. No fast movement option for endgame 100% farming, which is a baffling design choice consider Luna Nights did have it.  Overall a good entry from Ladybug, though I think Luna Nights as a whole is the better game.

Lindo jogo. Me fez querer assistir a série, que por sinal tem aquele estilo maravilhoso de animação dos anos 80-90, como Vampire Hunter D.  Como jogo, Metroidvania bem direto, sem mta sala escondida, upgrades interessantes mas o que mais me interessou foram as mecânicas dos espíritos. As lutas se tornam bem dinâmicas com as trocas dos espíritos.  Os controles iniciais são japoneses, então o pulo é no círculo e ataque no X. Recomendo que troque para pulo no X, ataque no QUADRADO, magia no TRIÂNGULO e arco no O. Tirando os controles, o jogo não é difícil mas é uma história bem divertida. Super recomendo.  Aliás, magia e armas de longo alcance como bumerangue e chakram são extremamente fortes pelo seu alcance. Algumas lutas são mais fáceis com greatswords mas no grosso, essas armas te alavancam bastante. Não precisei de grind 1 segundo, fazendo o jogo ser bastante direto. Vale muito a pena pegar na promoção.

给大号家庭共享玩的，初见流程4.2小时，太短了。  剧情太低龄，不过我玩杀怪的游戏本身也不怎么看剧情，这对我影响不大。  地图大但探索奖励少，开局100血，打到结局的时候130血， 总共就给3个提升血量的道具。 武器种类多，但大部分都是换皮，一共就那么三四套攻击动作， 剩下的只有单纯的增加数值。 战斗方面，双色切换有点新意，但不多。 到后期也是个没什么卵用的机制，BOSS直接射紫激光，哪个颜色都不好使。   游戏总体难度不高，但这个眩晕机制我真的很想骂人，被BOSS眩晕了会无限续杯控到死， 被隐身小怪眩晕了会被无限续杯直到推出房间，配合出房间就刷怪的逆天机制，专治低血压，帮助玩家快速叠血怒。 被蜘蛛网粘住了都能按键摇开， 给眩晕加个摇开很难吗？  看到那个什么奇美拉的新作才想起来这游戏还没打完， 打完之后发现真的不咋滴，新作卖60块更是只有不到10小时的流程，我只能说幸亏没买。 东方月神夜我也不吃了，谁爱吃谁吃吧。  虽然打个差评，但勉强够得着及格线，捏着鼻子能玩下去。

全てにおいて丁度いいゲームでした ２Dアクション好きとしては大満足です  サクサク進むし、追加で能力が増えていきメトロイドヴァニアの要素も少々ありました。 かといってすごく探索が必要というわけではないのでライトユーザーの方でも直感でわかるレベルではないでしょうか。  レベル上げも必要なく、道中の敵を倒してアイテム回収をしていくとボス戦までにちょうどいい感じのレベルに上がってくれます。ここらへんのバランスも本当に丁度よかったです。   シナリオとして ロードス島戦記の内容を知っていることが前提ですが、知らなくても何となく世界観に入っていけるのではないでしょうか 知らなくても何となくわかりそうな雰囲気は感じました  武器システムについて 弓とメイン武器の２種でメイン武器は剣などがありますが、振りの速度や範囲が違ってくるので好みで良いかと 店売りのものは殆ど買わず、道中で拾える装備がメインで使用しましたね （実績解除のために後に全部買いますが）  一部の武器についてはドロップ品になりますが、本当に落とすのかというレベルで出ない モンスター図鑑などでドロップする的は分かりますが、ドロップ率はシビアでした とはいっても数時間程度で回収は可能です   実績解除については武器集めが大半を締めます マップ踏破率は気がついたら１００％になるのでそこまで根を詰めなくても良かったかな [spoiler] QRコードぽいのがあり、読み込んだら隠し部屋のヒントが出てきたときはビビりましたけどね [/spoiler]  ボリュームは控えめでしたが、価格帯やプレイ内容を考えれば良いゲームです 逆にガッツリ楽しみたいって人には向いてないかもしれません 少しの時間アクションを楽しみたいっていう場合は最高に向いていると思います      ２Dでも分かる美人を操作するのは最高なんすわ ピロテースも最高、エルフは正義

Хорошая метроидвания. Балдежные графон и музыка, хорошо продуманный геймплей. Мало именно классического брожения поисков, больше чисто бектрекинг сбегай влево потом вправо потом снова влево. Последняя глава безумно душная, зачем-то в каждую комнату было напихано по пять противнков у которых какое-то невменяемое количество хп. Ну и финальные боссы тоже перекрученные. Вначале всё было круто, чувствуешь что просто за счёт понимания механик и паттернов вывозишь всегда. Под конец начинаются прекрасные станлоки, невидимые противники стоящие поверх других здоровенных противников, которые при касании оглушают тебя. Ещё и сохранения вдобавок к этому забыли поставить, в начале за каждым углом сейв + телепорт, в конце половину главы пройди без сейва, и ведь не зачизить никак, противники возрождаются при перезаходе в комнату. Особенно это приятно если тебя просто выталкивают из комнаты и проходи теперь заново.  Поставил бы выше оценку, если бы не дикая душка в конце, которая натурально просто попортила удовольствие от игры.

Great game from start to finish. Some of the best 2.5D graphics I've seen with smooth transitions and animations. The layered effects on the environmental backgrounds and bosses were a nice touch. Some of the best boss fights I've had in a game like this for sure. Amazing art style! Gameplay was fast and controls are very responsive. The Mogura Engine 2 is an absolute treat and I will now seek out more games that make full use of it. While a couple tracks stood out above the rest, overall the OST was nothing extremely memorable to me. The map design was lacking in uniqueness and quite uninspired. More layers and complexity was definitely needed. Enemies were varied in design but their attacks left me wanting. The entire game was rather easy. Despite these shortcomings, I still think the game was fun and definitely worth it's low cost of entry. Team Ladybug make high quality games for such low prices.
Very good game!!!

This is a high quality metroidvania. There's a huge variety of weapons to use and the Ikaruga-inspired main mechanic, quickly changing your elemental affinity to absorb incoming attacks, is very fluid and makes for engaging boss battles. If the gameplay being great isn't enough for you, the gorgeous backgrounds and fluid combat animations make up some of the best pixel art around.  Only two downsides are that the game is unfortunately a bit short (although it's about the same size as some of the genre's classics like SotN and Super Metroid) and a tad more linear than it could be. But make no mistake, this is a very good game!

This is the first game I've ever played that actually pulls off fluid "battle-mage" gameplay. You feel like an elf, you move and hit and cast like an elf. It's really well done! I beat it in about 5 hours - It doesn't drag. The story is all a bit cryptic and very brainy - it feels like a direct translation from japanese. I wish it had more of the Golden Age of Tabletop RPGs feel that the OVA had. You never really have much interaction with the game other than fighting things and unlocking doors and movement. This game was laser focused and I can appreciate that. It's fun and also deedlit is my wife.
this is a marvelous metroidvania. gorgeous pixel art, great story and music. very good and fun combat system  also a lot of weapons to collect 11/10

1. 개인평 로도스도 전기는 80~90년대에 탄생한 판타지 명작이라.. 저도 제목이랑 여자 엘프만 알지 따로 접해본 작품은 아니예요. 하지만 여엘프 빠로서, 현시대까지 금발 여엘프의 영향을 미친 엘프 디자인이 나오는데 당연히 해야 하는 것 아닙니까!!   장르는 매트로베니아에 7~8시간이면 엔딩을 볼 수 있습니다. 게임성도 깔끔하게 잘 빠졌고요. 제가 매트로베니아 장르를 많이 해보지 않아서 타게임과 비교하기는 어렵지만, 엔딩 볼때 까지 꽤나 재미있게 플레이 했습니다. 숨겨진 길, 마법, 무기 수집, 능력 해금으로 못 가는 맵 가기 등등!! 기본적으로 즐길거리는 다 있는 듯 해요. 만약 게임 하실 거면 스토리적인 부분은 유튜브에 로도스도 전기 요약 해놓은 영상들이 있으니 꼭 보시는걸 추천 드립니다.   다만 작은 볼륨을 감안하면 할인 할 때 구매하는 게 좋을 것 같네요. 혹은 연휴나 주말에 가볍게 엔딩 하나 보고 싶은 게임이 있자면 이 게임이 적당한 것 같습니다. 아니면 엘프빠다!!! 그냥 사서 플레이 하세요~~!!!   2. 도전과제 도전과제 난이도는 보통입니다. 똥손이 제가 그나마 좀 걸린 건 '화살과녁 S등급' 받는 거 였는데, 공략 유튜브 보고 진짜 여러번 해서 실패 하다가 또 운빨크리 터져서 클리어함... ㅜ.ㅜ 나머진 유튜브에 공략 찾아보시면 쉽게 달성 할 수 있습니다.  아!! 맞다. '무기 수집' 도전과제 하실거면.. '판의 검'이라고 조건이 최종 보스 1페이즈에서 한 대로 안 맞고 클리어 해야 하는데, 이 조건 달성으로 '판의 검'이 나오면 2페이즈 돌입 전에 꼭 획득하세요. 전 2페이즈 끝나고 얻으려다가 바로 엔딩 넘어가서... 보스 2번이나 꺴어요.. ㅠ.ㅠ  '보스러쉬 S등급'은 유튜브 보면 대부분 영상이 99 레벨 찍고, 어마 무시한 속도로 클리어 하는데, 저는 67레벨에 판의 검 들고 스톤 마법 난사하면서 4분 50초에 최종 보스까지 클리어 하니 S등급 달성 했어요~ 굳히 99레벨 노가다 안 하셔도 될 듯 하니, 참고하셔서 즐거운 도전과제 라이프를 즐기세욧!!!

Сюжет конкретно игры (а не всего аниме) 3\10 , просто чтобы у героини была мотивация, думаю убрать его особо сильно ничего не изменится.

Честно игра очень понравилась, как будто какая-то ностальгия их детства когда я играл в серию игр Castlevania на Gameboy Advance, а в особенности часть Aria of Sorrow. Этим меня игра и зацепила, бегаешь, ищешь, сражаешься как в те времена.  Игра по сюжетной составляющей конечно не всем подойдёт, для этого вам нужно ознакомится с источниками (манга и аниме). Но мне всё понравилось в этом плане)  По геймлею как всегда задушился только в самом конце, так как фарм всех луков и мечей (в особенности за ласт босса и за ранг S на полигоне) меня выбесили, так и было еще в детстве с Castlevania - ну такой минимальный шанс выпадения это просто уххх п****ц.  Если кому-то не хватило основной игры, фармёжки лута с мобов - то в конце игра вам открывает арену где вы должны будете зафармить всех боссов одним раном чтобы получить ранги B - A - и конечно же aSS :^)  В общем, что я хочу сказать, спасибо за приятные воспоминания разрабам и вообще создателям сие шедевра про Дидлит, всем советую игру, в особенности по ностальгии таких игр :)

It's not a crazy long game.  It's a nicely built metroidvania RPG, with a fun magic system where your primary maigc is fire or ice.  If you have fire selected, and the incoming magic is also fire, you absorb that magic.  It's awesome.  I actually beat several bosses using the invisibility magic, by absorbing incoming attacks carefully, and just staying out of the normal attack range and shooting my bow.  Most of the bosses I beat the normal way, by combining melee attacks and looking at the weakness list and using my magic to target that weakness.  You don't need to remember the anime to enjoy the game.
Really fun

This is a pretty good metroidvania with some weaknesses in terms of level design. Unfortunately there aren't too many hidden secrets (and most of them are marked by cracks in the wall that are fairly easy to spot) and the game is pretty short (~6 hours to beat all the stages, ~8 hours for 100% completion), but tight controls, great boss design and beautiful graphics make up for the game's shortfalls. Definitely check this out if you like the genre.

Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth is a classic SNES-style metroidvania game set in the Lodoss of War world. We control Deedlit, a High elf who mysteriously appears in the Wonder Labyrinth as she attemps to uncover why she suddenly appeared there as well as why her former companions behave in an odd way.  The gameplay itself is fairly simple and the entire game relies on the gimmick of switching between two elements: wind and fire, which could wear off if the game wasn't so short. The enemies, bosses and the very few challenges there are are not very hard either.  The game is short, even if you attempt to 100% it shouldn't take more than 9-10 hours, and I mean 100% it, not only get all achievements (Although in this case, they're the one and same).  Overall, if you want a somewhat succint, classic, metroidvania experience, this game could be your thing.

Castlevania SoTN fans out there: this game is what you've been looking for. Map exploration with back-tracking, great boss fights with move-patterns, spells, various weapons, all of that and much more. As a metroidvania fan, I really loved this game.  My overall score: 4.7 / 5 --- Super recommended. Definitely buy it if you like metroidvanias.  Gameplay: 5/5 This game is great. The elements mechanis and the spells are great, they make you think in order to beat the enemies. The bow and arrow puzzles are also good. Boss fights are one of the best I've ever seen, probably the best part of the game. The only thing I missed is a better menu for changing equips and spells, and maybe having more equips.  Graphics: 5/5 Nice pixel art, great animation, beautiful scenarios. A real spiritual successor to SoTN.  Story: 4/5 Great story, worth watching every cutscene and reading all dialogues.  Replayability: 4/5 I still have to play it again to get the achievements I'm missing and 100% the map. So there's great potential for replaying it.  Music: 5/5 Great music. Again JRPGs at their best.  PC Requirements: 5/5 A toaster will do (seriously, this game is really light).  Multiplayer: It's a single player game, so it doesn't apply.

先說結論： Team Ladybug很會做遊戲！ 而且都會突破傳統遊戲框架，讓玩家有耳目一新的體驗： 東方月神夜是玩時間暫停，Drainus是玩吸收反擊，而這款是玩切換屬性做攻防。  本作最大的缺點是，劇情上對於沒接觸過⟨羅德斯島戰記⟩的玩家非常不友善，我玩了接近5小時下來，只知道遊戲操作上很有趣，但劇情完全看不懂，每個角色對話都只講一半，人物彼此關係也都不懂，看來劇情入門門檻有點高。 如果遇到每一位角色都能像開頭一樣有一個小畫面交代一下女主與戀人的關係，這樣會比較好懂。  大約7小時可全破遊戲（不含成就） 是個有挑戰性的動作遊戲，畫面美術音樂也很有帶入感。 劇情上對於不懂羅德斯島戰記的人來說，就是一個糾結人變得不糾結的過程。  1/16會買幻刃奇美拉，玩過試玩版讓我再次驚艷！
感受一下开山的日式西幻和寿命论 玩下来味很正，难度很亲民，就全收集了

Record of Lodoss War-Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth to całkiem niezła gra spod szyldu metroidvania cechująca się ciekawymi rozwiązaniami gameplay'owymi. Jeśli jesteś weteranami gatunki, to bez problemu się w niej odnajdziecie. Mapa do zwiedzania jest sporo, nie brakuje ukrytych lub zablokowanych przejść, a bestiariusz prezentuje się całkiem okazale. Bossowi potrafią sprawić graczowi sporo frustracji, a co za tym idzie, stanowią wyzwanie i żeby ich pokonać nie wystarczy katowanie dwóch lub trzech klawiszy odpowiedzialnych za atak. Sam fabuła jest dość pretekstowa, lecz potrafi zaskoczyć, szczególnie im bliżej jesteśmy końca. Ja mogę powiedzieć, iż serdecznie polecam Record of Lodoss.

Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth is a solid metroidvania experience. Althought it doesn't bring anything new to the table, it does everything right for the genre. Game is kinda short, took me 8 hours to complete (not 100%), but it was worth every minute. I just felt since it takes so much from Symphony of the Night, it should excel on everything that made SotN good and add even more, like more weapons, enemies, biomes, magic, etc. But the game is like half of what SotN is. But still, it is worth it.  And I wasn't expecting this to feel like a closure to the Record of the Lodoss War series, and any fan of the anime will probably feel the same. Can't say much, or I will spoil the end, but trust me, it is good.  You will like it, just like I "Deed".

I didn't watch Record of Lodoss War before playing this game but after being thrilled with the story and characters I'll be watching it now. I had no idea who Parn or Deedlit or Ashram were before starting this game and but I want to fight out for sure.   A blast to play through with a lot of cool gimmicks core to the gameplay throughout the fight, the final bosses can be rather challenging too and basically require you to explore the map and find every single spirit magic ever, it rewards players who are flexible and adapt their tactics on the fly.   Great artstyle, amazing music, and a complete steal if it's on sale.   If you love Castlevania, Pseudoregalia, and/or Hollow Knight, or if you love Record of Lodoss War, buy this game.   The fact that is directly continues from an classic and amazing anime too makes this even more of a steal.  Toward the end I really felt like a badass magic-wielding warrior elf using ancient weapons and commanding spirits to aid me in battle. The entire concept for the game's story is genius too, it's rare you find a game that actually explains in-game mechanics and weave them into the story.   Enemy variety goes CRAZY in this game. You can beat the whole game without encountering some enemies.   A real hidden gem, play this game NOW.  ...but probably watch Record of Lodoss War first for the best experience.

This was a pretty good castlevania-like experience. I don't know the franchise so the story didn't make much sense to me, but sense of importance in it and the interesting world kept me going even when sections felt like a slog. Some areas were a bit confusing and the backtracking was an issue at times, but the simpler puzzles and tight controls made navigation pretty smooth. I also liked the two-element system, as it added to the experience without over-complicating the controls or systems, as more elements would have. The game does not overstay its welcome, and I'm interested to see how things improve further in their newer games.

I didn't really know what to expect from the game, I bought it because of Lodoss and because it looked beautiful.  When I started the game I had some SotN feelings and I knew the game was going to be good. You have different types of weapons which change the attack animation (reach, speed...) and magic, but the big gameplay innovation is the switch between the two spirits that give you each one particular move for the platforming, one weakness/resistance against mobs, and a life regen system. Bosses and music are nice too. The only bad point is that you can clear the game in less than 6 hours, which is a little bit short, but the game is not so expensive to begin with so...

Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth es bastante reminiscente a Castlevania Symphony of the Night, lo cual se puede notar desde el primer momento ya que todo parece inspirado de ese juego, incluso el movimiento mismo de los personajes. Dicho esto, de igual forma es bastante interesante y la progresión es satisfactoria, con diseños de nivel que permiten al jugador darse cuenta rápidamente de los elementos para utilizar determinada habilidad durante la exploración y no quedarse "estancado". El diseño de los personajes es idéntico a lo visto previamente en Lodoss War y se ven bastante bien adaptados al pixel art, lo mismo para los monstruos y los jefes. En general es una experiencia algo corta pero que al finalizar deja una sensación agradable por haberlo concluido.  Su único punto negativo importante es su posición cronológica con respecto a Lodoss War, ya que se espera que el jugador tenga conocimiento de ciertos acontecimientos de Record of Lodoss War (aunque si se vio alguna de las dos adaptaciones animadas, se puede tener al menos una vaga noción). Si bien alguien puede entrar a la saga con este juego, narrativamente se perderá de muchos momentos o guiños que representan la parte emocional de la excelente historia y su conclusión.
Great, fairly short, metroidvania with a unique battle system and great pixel art. The story is easy to follow and understand even if you haven't seen the anime (which I haven't)

It's okay.  It seems fair to compare it to SoTN because it's clear that's what the game was going for. And no, not just by virtue of being a "Metroidvania." I'm talking a full-on, walking, breathing, literal *homage* to SoTN in every respect. Which should sound exciting because SoTN is so iconic. But...  There's a problem. They thought if they could copy the sound effects, mechanics, and even run cycle of SoTN 1-to-1 then that would make this game equally as good. But they missed the soul of what made SoTN so amazing. It wasn't the graphics or arguably even the gameplay. It was the atmosphere. Each map you explored was rich with detailed backgrounds and an amazing soundtrack to draw you into every inch of Dracula's castle. You wanted to explore the whole thing because every area had so much imagination. But here? These maps are mostly lifeless. There isn't a single area in the game that I can even remember. I vaguely recall some garden courtyard place, but it's rather forgettable.  The combat is mostly fine. They added in an Ikaruga thing where you switch between two colours to absorb shots of the same colour. That's all fine and good, but they made the mistake of putting too much ♥♥♥♥ on the screen so you don't know when something's coming. I'll be unloading all my arrows into a boss while also absorbing energy from the flames on the ground while also having my familiar blast fireballs at him and then all of a sudden I get hit by one of his projectiles-- which I couldn't even see coming because there's already too many hit sparks cluttering up the screen. The bosses also don't get staggered by your attacks in the slightest bit, so as much as you want to run up to them and throw hands, it's just not worth the risk. You'll find yourself spending every battle maximizing the distance between you and the bad guy so you can blast him with arrows and spells rather than closing the distance to risk some melee combat. This seems... not what these games are supposed to be about. I made it as far as the Incomplete Ashram (or whatever his name is) fight before turning the game off. I don't even really feel like finishing it.  Also, minor nitpick, but I couldn't be arsed to follow the story at all. I really didn't give af who these guys were and why they were at each other's throats half the time. I should mention I haven't watched the anime this game is based off of. But again, to bring it back to SoTN, you don't need to have played any previous Castlevanias to get drawn into the story. If I'm skipping the dialogue sections of new bosses I've never even seen before, then I think that's a bad sign. Less yapping, more showing please.  I feel bad writing so much negative stuff because I loved Team Ladybug's earlier work (Touhou Luna Nights) and there's actually a lot to love about this game as well (I mean, the spritework alone in in this game is cleeeeeaaaan. Some of the best I've seen). I also appreciate how they kept the combat commands to six buttons and they made all the menus super accessible and to-the-point instead of clogging it down with tedium like some other games tend to do. But all of those conveniences can't fix a game that's frankly mid overall.  I feel like a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ leaving such a lukewarm review because I know Team Ladybug is only a small handful of people and I'm sure they worked their asses off making this. But at the end of the day, I have to ask myself: do I even feel like picking this game up again and beating it? The answer is no, probably not. Sorry again.

Record of Lodoss War -Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth- é uma carta de amor não apenas à série Record of Lodoss War, mas também ao gênero metroidvania. Desenvolvido pela Team Ladybug, o jogo é uma experiência belíssima e cativante que combina combate fluido, mecânicas interessantes e uma direção artística que é um verdadeiro deleite visual.  Você assume o papel de Deedlit, a elfa alta e graciosa, explorando um misterioso labirinto cheio de segredos, inimigos e puzzles. O jogo começa de forma intrigante, sem revelar muito sobre como Deedlit foi parar nesse lugar, mas vai apresentando a narrativa aos poucos, com diálogos que remetem diretamente ao rico universo de Record of Lodoss War. Para quem é fã da franquia, é um prato cheio, com várias referências e aparições de personagens clássicos.  No quesito jogabilidade, Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth se destaca. O combate é extremamente fluido e viciante, com mecânicas que lembram clássicos como Castlevania: Symphony of the Night. O diferencial, no entanto, está no sistema de troca de espíritos. Deedlit pode alternar entre dois espíritos elementais, Sylph (vento) e Salamander (fogo), e cada um oferece habilidades únicas que são cruciais tanto para o combate quanto para resolver puzzles. Essa mecânica adiciona uma camada estratégica ao jogo, especialmente quando você precisa se adaptar rapidamente às fraquezas dos inimigos ou usar os elementos para evitar danos.  A progressão é muito satisfatória, com novas armas, habilidades e upgrades sendo encontrados ao longo da jornada. Há uma boa variedade de armas para experimentar, cada uma com seu próprio estilo de combate, permitindo que você personalize a jogabilidade ao seu gosto. O level design também é excelente, com mapas que incentivam a exploração sem nunca se tornarem frustrantes.  Visualmente, o jogo é uma obra de arte. Os sprites em 2D são incrivelmente detalhados, com animações suaves e cenários lindamente desenhados. Cada área tem sua própria identidade visual, tornando a exploração ainda mais recompensadora. A trilha sonora complementa perfeitamente a experiência, com músicas atmosféricas e emocionantes que capturam a essência do universo de Lodoss.  Para os fãs do gênero metroidvania, Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth é um título obrigatório. Ele entrega uma experiência compacta, mas incrivelmente polida, que respeita o tempo do jogador. Apesar de sua duração ser relativamente curta, em torno de 6 a 8 horas, cada minuto é bem aproveitado, sem enchimentos desnecessários.  Por fim, seja você um fã de longa data de Record of Lodoss War ou alguém que apenas gosta de bons jogos de ação e exploração, Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth é uma joia que merece ser jogada. É uma experiência que consegue ser nostálgica e moderna ao mesmo tempo, e mostra como o amor e dedicação de um time de desenvolvedores podem fazer toda a diferença.

[h1]蒂德莉特的奇境冒险   不仅仅是粉丝向[/h1] 玩过今年的动物井和波斯王子：失落的王冠后实在是把我的胃口养刁了，那些普普通通没什么特点的类银河恶魔城游戏已经是完全玩不下去了，直到遇到了这款21年的粉丝向游戏并且让我大吃一惊。  [h1]粉丝向却不是gal?[/h1] 一个粉丝向的游戏居然没有选择做galgame，毕竟像《罗德岛战记》这种作品改编galgame在日本已经是很成熟的方案了。为什么制作组要选择类银河恶魔城这个更加吃功底且原作粉丝还不一定买账的品类？而在游完之后我终于得到了答案，制作组的底气来源于他们出色的类银河恶魔城的制作功底，我这种完全没有看过原著的非粉丝玩家都玩的十分舒服。虽然看不懂剧情但一点不影响游戏的推进，游戏的地图在获得新能力前往往只有一两条路线可以走，在充分探索后获得新的能力后又可以顺势前往之前到不了的区域，整个探索过程非常自然，没有出现过找不到路的情况，后期也是通过不同的门来限制地图，在击败固定boss后就会开启，这时刚好探索完其他区域来这里又可以解锁新的地图。  [h1]独特的战斗体验[/h1] 游戏的战斗和boss设计也让我惊艳，游戏没有血瓶这一道具，而取代他的是火精灵与风精灵三级后可以回血的机制。不过积累精灵等级需要携带另一个精灵时攻击击杀怪物，这就要求玩家多多切换精灵战斗，而且切换还有很多好处，有的怪弱火有的怪弱风，鼓励玩家多多尝试切换，有时会发现意想不到的效果。如果仅仅是这样那也就是还算有新意的系统，但制作组将精灵更加深入的融合进了游戏的战斗系统。在boss战中，boss往往会使用两种属性攻击或是一种属性攻击+一种物理攻击的方式，切换为与boss属性攻击相同的精灵就可以直接免疫伤害，而且不同的boss还有不同的属性免疫，需要玩家在战斗过程中频繁的切换精灵来克制boss，大大增加了游戏的操纵上限与乐趣。  [h1]非粉丝剧情略[/h1] 剧情方面的话我也没有看过原作就不过多赘述了（反正也看不懂），毕竟银河战士不也就是一路寻找仇敌最后干掉的剧情，这种游戏只要引导做好看不看的懂剧情完全无所谓，而像有些游戏剧情很简单要你去某一个地方，但死活找不到路才是最痛苦的了。  [h1]总结[/h1] 本作即便在众多的类银河恶魔城中也是独一份的体验，改编自小说的剧情原作粉丝狂喜，不过看不懂也不要紧，火风精灵切换的战斗系统，四通八达的地图在类银游戏中都算得上是的上上等的，如果你也是玩腻了那些知名的类银游戏，不如来试试这一款一定不会让你失望。  [quote]关注「 [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/42276832//]鸿源鉴游组」 [/url] [/quote] [u][b] 会当逢佳游，始可见鸿源。 [/b][/u]

Record of lodos war deadlit in wonder labyrinth was a deceptively fun metroidvania experience that nails the feeling of older games without the clunkiness of said games. This game has perfect controls that feel smooth and satisfying to use from beginning till end. While that won't take you a long time, anywhere from 4-7 hours depending on player skill and completion percentage, it'll be over before you know it. That is because this game is just addictive with its combat, slashing through enemies in a few hits never gets old and it's kinda ridiculous how strong you can become. Every new weapon you find is a huge jump in power allowing you to obliterate previous enemies while leveling the playing field against what comes. The whole game has a great progression curve that constantly maintains the challenge while never being difficult. What i mean by that is that you can destroy most enemies but you still have to use your brain, so patience is key. This is especially true for bosses which are the definitive highlight of the game.  Each boss in this game is tons of fun, with unique & memorable designs and attack patterns. Just brute forcing your way through is not possible but you can quickly learn the attack patterns and overcome each boss within 10 minutes given a few deaths to learn the ropes. Every attack is properly telegraphed and can be dodged in it's own way. Especially the elemental attacks are fun to dodge because you can absorb them with the proper element. Your character deedlit can instantly swap between two stances, those being fire and wind, with fire able to absorb fire attacks and wind absorbing wind attacks. Bosses make great use of this mechanic keeping you on your toes to swap accordingly. They also fire regular attacks that require jumping, dashing or sliding to avoid so there's no universal solution which keeps fights interesting and dynamic. The way you heal in this game is also very unique and cool, because fighting in one stance makes the other more powerful with the third level being the most powerful.   On the 3rd level you deal the most damage and automatically heal but taking a hit knocks you back down and taking several sets you back to level one, forcing you to swap to the other stance to build the 1st one back up. Balancing your stances, ideally keeping both at lvl 3, whilst avoiding enemy attacks is suprisingly engaging. And while there are very usefull healing potions that you can buy for cheap the game is more fun if you rely on these stances for the most part, only using potions in a pickle. If you properly manage your stances you can stay on top, but if you take hits as both you are running dry on heals and things can escalate from there. Fighting back to lvl 3 on both in a sticky situation is very satisfying and i ended some bosses with only a sliver of health left. This paired with how fun weapons feel to use and how powerfull your magic spells or bow are made combat super fun both against bosses or regular enemies. Speaking of the bow, this was for one quick & fun to use in combat, but more importantly also used to solve some puzzles.   While said puzzles were relatively easy they still served as a fun way to break up the combat because you're fighting 80% of the time in this game. There's only a bit of platforming but nothing noteworthy. What's there though is fun due to the smooth movement. Level design is mostly linear but there's some branching paths and finding a stronger new weapon feels very rewarding, which also made exploration more fun. There aren't crazy things to see but each place looks nice and the pixel art is very pretty to look at. While nothing jumps out or is too memorable the whole still makes for an interesting world. This is also true for the story which intrigued me despite having no former knowledge of the lodos war lore. Not that this game explains a lot but i liked deedlit and her quest. Finally the game ran smoothly and was bug free so if you like fun combat that isn't too hard but not mindless either, and don't mind basic exploration  then this game will entertain you with it's smooth movement, fun bosses and intrigueing setting all complemented by a properly retro soundtrack.

[h3][b]ー『妥協』ー[/b][/h3]  ドット絵ながら綺麗なグラフィック、心地よい音楽、ストレスのない操作性。 非常に商品としてのツボを押さえたゲーム作りで開発は良い仕事をしていると思う。種類の多い武器、隠し部屋、ミニゲームなど探索し甲斐のある丁度良い広さのマップ。TRPG由来のサイコロ演出。名作シューティング「斑鳩」を思わせる属性切り替えによる敵の攻撃の無効化システム。 ゲームとして見るべき点は多い。  しかし、圧倒的にゲームとしての魅力が足りない。 原因はこのゲームの根幹部分が良く分からないのが大きな理由なんだと思う。例えばどんなに綺麗な服を着ていようが、どんなに歌が上手かろうが、どんなに社交性が高かろうが、そいつがクソ野郎なら大して意味もないようなものではないだろうか。 別にこのゲームがクソゲーと言いたいわけではない。むしろどちらかと言えば面白いゲームだろう。 中心部分がちゃんとしてないだけだ。  メトロイドヴァニアとして特別目を引くような点は感じられないし、ゲームのボリュームも7時間もあれば充分クリアできるような内容で正直物足りない。武器や魔法が非常に強く、システム面も強いので縛りプレイでもしないと攻略の手応えも感じにくいだろう。多彩な攻撃で作り込まれていそうなボス戦でさえ、ランダム要素が少なくパターン化しやすいので慣れたら作業になりやすい。 プレイヤーはこのゲームの一体どこに独自の価値を感じたらいいのだろうか。 何より一番中心に据えるべき「ロードス島戦記」の魅力が原作を知らない人には伝わってこないのが致命的過ぎる。ボイスもなければ一枚絵もないのでアニメ当時の表現や主人公のディードリットの美しさがまったく表現されていない。 ストーリーは原作を知らなくても全く関係ないのが分かるくらいどうでもいい出来である。 累計1000万部越えの人気作の「無駄遣い」と言われても仕方がないだろう。 ただ、弓矢を使ったパズル要素は頭を使ったのでいくらかやりごたえがあった。登場人物やボスなどのグラフィックは原作再現度は高そうなので、その点も原作ファンなら嬉しいかもしれない。  そこそこ楽しめるし悪いゲームでは決してないが、開発の技術力の高さが感じられる作品なだけに「もっとやれただろう」と言わずにはいられない、悪い意味で「妥協」したゲームとして筆者は評価したいと思います。  [h3][b]【レビュー評価　6/10 】[/b][/h3] [table]     [tr]         [th]原作のゲーム化[/th]         [th]3[/th]     [/tr]     [tr]         [td]魅力[/td]         [td]3[/td]     [/tr]     [tr]         [td]グラフィック[/td]         [td]8[/td]     [/tr]     [tr]         [td]とっつきやすさ[/td]         [td]8[/td]     [/tr] [/table] [h3][b]【レビュー動画（ずんだもん＆松嘩りすく）】[/b][/h3] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PY760LEJqi8&ab_channel=%E3%81%99%E3%81%B7%E3%83%BC

Finally beat it. Got it in early access because [strike]cute elf[/strike] I really liked Symphony of the Night. Ultimately, me having no clue what a "Lodoss" is turned me away from the game half-way through. Now that I have a basic idea what a Lodoss is, I returned to it, and I finished it. And it's good. I won't comment on the story because my understanding of it is still very fragile, I have no idea who anything except Deedlit is, so I'll focus on the gameplay.  Much like Unepic, Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth is a metroidvania, but less focused on roleplaying and with a more arcade-like, straightforward gameplay. You don't have any quest beside "move forward", and you cannot turn off upgrades because you will need them. You pick a melee weapon and a bow, and there are some variants (like one that shoots five arrows at once, another bow with homing arrows), but my impression is that the game is mostly about spells and magic, rather than hit things in the face until they die. Meaning, that, for a large part of the game, you will feel powerless, until you get the spells that you need to exploit enemies weaknesses. And by the way. Blue dices are weaknesses, red dices are resistances. Woohpey.  I don't think there's more to say: story is pure fanservice and won't explain anything if you don't know the base material, but the gameplay is extremely solid, through most of the game feels like a tutorial until you get enough spells to abuse the enemies' elemental weaknesses.

This is not like Symphony of the Night. It looks similar and has similar base mechanics but you will not have a nice relaxing time with this one. If you have fast reflexes and enjoy platformers, then this is for you. The main gameplay mechanic is switching between elements on the fly and getting hit will severely reduce your attack power - until you build it up again by hitting enemies with the opposite element. Once again - THIS IS NOT LIKE SYMPHONY OF THE NIGHT. It's a good game that a lot of work clearly went into but it is NOT casual and you WILL have a bad time playing it with half a brain (like me) - good for some, not for others.

首发为了恶魔城风格和ELF的XP入的，但最近才发觉在发售打通关后更新了成就系统，开始刷之后又重新完整体验了一遍，还是那个味道，非常贴近月下夜想曲的美术和手感，难度也适中；  动画我只看过几集当年的OVA，但这个ELF的经典角色却属实通过后续的其他致敬与约定俗成烙印进了我的XP里；游戏剧情里还有一股忧伤的寿命论基调，最后的最后貌似还有点留白；总体不失为一个好游戏，除了那个最变态的靶场S评价的成就…

Um exótico tributo para Record of Lodoss.  É mais um metroidvania, que não apenas pouco foge do genérico, mas pode-se dizer que quase copia por completo Castlevania Symphony of the Night, porém ele busca inovar com algumas mecânicas inéditas.  Sendo sincero, o game é mediano em basicamente tudo. Acho que as melhores partes são tua trilha sonora que se destaca pela qualidade, e também a grande variedade de inimigos, que ajudam na transição dos cenários e seções do jogo.  A gameplay é muito agradável e funcional; apresenta um vasto arsenal de equipamentos, que apesar de ser possível escolher armas específicas para determinadas situações, é feito de modo que haja uma progressão gradual obrigatória. Há também magias poderosas, que variam o gameplay e adicionam uma pitada de estratégia para os combates (bem pouca mesmo).  Os puzzles, apesar de simples, exercem bem tua função. De primeiro encontro, a resolução teórica é simples, mas na prática requer um pouco de destreza para concluí-los.  Quanto a qualidade do enredo, eu diria que é algo muito subjetivo. Eu particularmente gostei, porque é uma narrativa que explora o subconsciente da personagem principal, com situações que as vezes podem ou não ser apenas uma ilusão, e que se explica ao decorrer da progressão da história. Mas nem tudo é tão lindo e coerente; o final é completamente ilógico e viajado. Ele busca um desfecho marcante e intenso, mas apenas consegue um amargor inesperado e um tumulto sem propósito.  Temos personagens icônicas marcando presença, alguns com funções diretamente relacionadas ao jogador, como um lojista, e outros que se mostram obstáculos em nossa busca por respostas.  A maioria está bem caracterizada, outros se mostram um tanto excêntricos por conveniência de roteiro ou função.  No fim, é um jogo que podia entregar muito mais e com ainda mais qualidade. Não é uma total perda de tempo, mas vale o questionamento do porquê jogá-lo. Para fãs do anime, é aquela migalha que almejamos para satisfazer o desejo de rever velhos amigos. Recomendo, mas com ressalvas.  Importante salientar que o jogo pressupõe que o player conheça e já tenha consumido a obra original, pois há várias menções diretas aos eventos do mesmo ao longo da jornada.

Pretty good. Team Ladybug has only a few releases, but they're all pretty polished to a T. This one isn't as ambitious, comparitively, as Touhou Luna Nights (their other metroidvania), but what it does, it does pretty well. Very SOTN-inspired, which is a weird thing to say considering the entire genre takes after SOTN, but there are visual cues that are familiar in this, mainly in Deedlit's character sprite, her movement and even the little ghostly aura she leaves behind when moving; very Alucard.  It's the usual affair for this type of game; hallways packed with enemies, connected through different doors, which you have to unlock over time, a bit of exploration to pick up more weapons and spells. It's not terribly rich in content, there's only a couple of instances that you need a new move or power-up to unlock more of the map (making this more -vania than metroid-), but it's a fairly short game anyway. Controls are nice and fluid, combat feels good for the most part, as does traversal. The most unique mechanic is the requirement to shift between two magical spirits for some boss and enemy encounters, as well as some light level traversal "puzzles".  The story is just kind of there. The game assumes you're already familiar with at least the Record of Lodoss War OVA from 1990. If you're not, you'll be completely lost. If you are, the story is a nothingburger, but you get to at least enjoy the cameos.   The sprite art is great and the music is effective. This type of thing is Ladybug's bread and butter, so no surprises there.  Overall, it's a nice and relaxing little metroidvania. It doesn't do anything new, but what it does, it does well. It's not too challenging, it's not braindead either, it looks and sounds good, it plays well. The asking price might be a little too much, considering the competition, but it's worth it if you pick it up during a sale.

É um jogo de ação e aventura em estilo Metroidvania, desenvolvido pela Team Ladybug e lançado em 2021, que mergulha os jogadores em uma experiência nostálgica e visualmente impressionante. Situado no universo de Record of Lodoss War, o jogo coloca os jogadores na pele de Deedlit, uma elfa guerreira que explora um labirinto repleto de combates e segredos. A arte em pixel destaca-se pela atenção minuciosa aos detalhes e animações fluídas, recriando a atmosfera da fantasia clássica dos anos 90 com fidelidade.  A jogabilidade se baseia na exploração de cenários e no desenvolvimento de habilidades, com uma mecânica que permite alternar entre os elementos de fogo e vento, adicionando uma camada estratégica aos combates. Embora o design dos níveis seja relativamente linear, o jogo apresenta uma narrativa objetiva, enquanto a trilha sonora, apesar de discreta, reforça o clima imersivo. Por outro lado, a curta duração (em torno de 5 a 6 horas) e a limitada rejogabilidade podem frustrar jogadores que buscam um desafio mais duradouro.   concluindo-Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth é um encantador tributo nostálgico ao universo , sendo especialmente recomendado para fãs de fantasia e estética retrô.

A SEVERELY underrated Metroidvania game! It's Symphony of the Night with Ikaruga-style quick mode switching to absorb damage or power up your attacks and heal, keeping the action constant throughout. Even just playing the first 15 minutes gets you right into the good stuff with some of the best abilities to set the stage for you, you can make up your mind about it pretty fast - and you won't be disappointed. Great graphics, amazing anime artwork based on a cult classic anime, very memorable and interesting boss fights. My only gripes are that it's short, and the plot is razor thin, but the plot isn't that important anyway if you just came for the nostalgia and name-dropping anyway.

Un metroidvania sympathique, accessible aux débutants. Le jeu est plus simple que les autres jeux du genre, les seules difficultés étant les boss. Pour le reste, on retrouve tout ce qui est présent dans un Castelvania-like : équipements et armes nombreux et divers, un système de light-RPG avec des stats, la protagoniste récupère des pouvoirs au cours de l'aventure qui lui permettent de débloquer des portions de map, des téléporteurs pour back-tracker, etc.  Petite originalité : la protagoniste Deedlit a deux pouvoirs, un de feu et un d'eau, et peut en "équiper" un à la fois. Elle peut absorber les attaques ennemies si elles sont du même pouvoir, mais se les prend dans la tronche sinon. Toutes les attaques ne sont pas absorbées, les attaques physiques vous touchent systématiquement, mais on apprend rapidement à reconnaitre celles qui le sont. Les combats de boss font grand usage de cette mécanique, surtout pour les boss du début.  Un bémol cependant : la progression est très linéaire. On repasse rarement plusieurs fois au même endroit, le back-tracking est facultatif et ne sert qu'à trouver des bonus ou équipements en plus. Le jeu est pas loin d'être une ligne droite, on a juste certaines portes bloquées jusqu'à ce qu'on trouve un interrupteurs coloré, mais cela ne sert à qu'à débloquer l'accès à des bonus/armes. On est loin d'un Castlevania : Portrait of ruin ou qu'un SOTN.  Le jeu est assez court, le gros noob que je suis a atteint la fin en moins de 10 heures et sans galérer ailleurs que sur les boss. Il faut juste accepter de try hard les boss et faire le minimum syndical niveau apprentissage des patterns, rien de compliqué.  Maintenant, que vaut ce jeu en tant que jeu Lodoss ? Les personnages habituels reviennent, mais ils ont peu de dialogues. L'histoire est limitée à sa portion congrue, à savoir un strict minimum pour justifier que le jeu ait lieu. Les 6 "niveaux" ne sont pas aussi inspirés les uns que les autres.  Les environnements du début du jeu sont assez proches de ce qu'on attend d'un jeu orienté fantasy, mais le milieu ont une autre ambiance, ceux de fin ne collent pas du tout à du Lodoss. Les pouvoirs de la protagoniste sont eux dans le ton.  Je recommande le jeu à ceux qui aiment les Castlevania et qui acceptent de payer le prix du jeu pour 10 heures de gameplay grand max, mais j'ai quelques réserves pour ce qui est de le réserver aux fans de Lodoss (même aux gros gros fans qui veulent tout savoir de l'univers). Le jeu n'apporte pas grand chose à l'univers de Lodoss, ne raconte pas d'histoire, c'est juste une occasion d'incarner Deedlit.

Record of Lodoss War: Deedlit in Wonder Labyrinth is a atrocious name for an alright game. This is a 2D metroidvania that I have heard folks recommend as being similar to Symphony of the Night. I firmly disagree with that assessment but do think this game has some things going for it.   This game is based off the Record of Lodoss War franchise that I did not know anything of prior to playing this game. After the 6 hours of gameplay it took me to beat the final boss, I feel like I have no more firm a grasp as to the story or characters as when I began. Fans of the series probably will get a lot more out of this but I was completely lost. You control an elf named Deedlit who winds up in a castle populated by acquaintances both dead and alive. That's it- that's all I understood of the story and world.   The best part of this game by a wide margin is the combat. Deedlit can swap between using a wind or fire spirit as her familiar on the fly. The wind spirit gives Deedlit better traversal while the fire boosts her attack. As you attack an enemy with a given spirit attached, it builds up a meter for the opposite element. Once that meter is maximized,  you can switch to and slowly regain health in the process. While you lose meter each time you are hit, you can alternate elements to beat down an enemy, switch to heal and repeat. It forms a lovely cadence, especially during boss battles.   While this is a metroidvania which typically means that area of the map are gated off until you pick up a new traversal ability. This usually takes the form of a double jump, air dash or something of that ilk. In this game there is a bit of that, but mostly areas are literally gated off until you find the right color key. This isn't bad- just left me feeling disappointed at the lost potential. The one unique ability that this game has are shooting puzzles. Deedlit draws her bow and fires arrows into gear of sorts to unlock doors, move fan blades and reposition platforms. Unfortunately, I found the bow controls to be awkward and off-putting.   "Lost potential" does seem to be a recurring phrase in my head when thinking about this game. The sprite art is gorgeous but the backgrounds and music are so forgettable. The magic system is fun but the traversal is boring. The bow puzzles are unique but control poorly. This isn't a bad game- just a frustratingly uneven one. The one incredible part of this game is the combat system and I truly hope the devs use it in a less uneven game.   If there was an option to shrug instead of give a thumbs up or down, I'd give this game a shrug. I would recommend this game to folks who are familiar with the Record of Lodoss War series and/or prefer shorter games. At the price point of $20, there are so many better metroidvanias to play for that price or less, but I cannot deny that I had an enjoyable time with the game. It's that enjoyment of the combat system that gives it the slightest edge to recommend it, even with all the caveats.
